translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: ALIF LAM MIM.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Alif-Laam-Meem. (Alphabets of the Arabic language; Allah and to whomever He reveals, know their precise meanings.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Alif Lam Mim

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Alif. Lam Mim

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Alif-Lam-Mim. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Alif, Lam, Meem.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Alif. Lam. Mim.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Alif Lam Mim.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Alif. Lam. Mim.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Alif, Lam, Mim.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: AlifLaamMeem.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Alif, Lam, Meem.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Alif. Lam. Mim.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Alif Lam Mim.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Alif Lam Mim.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: A. L. M.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Aliflammeem

2.arabic: ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: This is The Book free of doubt and involution, a guidance for those who preserve themselves from evil and follow the straight path,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is the exalted Book (the Qur’an), in which there is no place for doubt; a guidance for the pious.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That is the Book, wherein is no doubt, a guidance to the godfearing

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This Book whereof there is no doubt, is a guidance unto the God-fearing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: This is the Book (the Quran), whereof there is no doubt, a guidance to those who are Al-Muttaqun [the pious and righteous persons who fear Allah much (abstain from all kinds of sins and evil deeds which He has forbidden) and love Allah much (perform all kinds of good deeds which He has ordained)].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: This is the Book in which there is no doubt, a guide for the righteous.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is the Book of Allah: there is no doubt about it. It is guidance to Godfearing people,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: That is Book in which there is no Rayb, guidance for the Muttaqin.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: This is the Scripture whereof there is no doubt, a guidance unto those who ward off (evil).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: This is the Book, there is no doubt in it, a guidance to the Godwary,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: That is the (Holy) Book, where there is no doubt. It is a guidance for the cautious (of evil and Hell).

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: This is the Book about which there is no doubt, a guidance for those conscious of Allah -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: There is no doubt that this book is a guide for the pious;

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: This Book, there is no doubt in it, is a guide to those who guard (against evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: This is the Book; there is no doubt in it. It is a guide for those who are mindful of God,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: This is the Book; in it is guidance sure, without doubt, to those who fear Allah;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thalika alkitabu la rayba feehi hudan lilmuttaqeena

3.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ وَيُقِيمُونَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who believe in the Unknown and fulfil their devotional obligations, and spend in charity of what We have given them;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Those who believe without seeing (the hidden), and keep the (obligatory) prayer established, and spend in Our cause from what We have bestowed upon them.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: who believe in the Unseen, and perform the prayer, and expend of that We have provided them;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Who believe in the Unseen, and establish prayer, and out of that wherewith We have provided them expend.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Who believe in the Ghaib and perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), and spend out of what we have provided for them [i.e. give Zakat, spend on themselves, their parents, their children, their wives, etc., and also give charity to the poor and also in Allah's Cause - Jihad, etc.].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who believe in the unseen, and perform the prayers, and give from what We have provided for them.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: who believe in the unseen, establish the Salats and expend (in Our way) out of what We have bestowed on them;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Those Who have faith in the Ghayb and perform Salah, and spend out of what we have provided for them.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Who believe in the Unseen, and establish worship, and spend of that We have bestowed upon them;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: who believe in the Unseen, maintain the prayer, and spend out of what We have provided for them;

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Who believe in the unseen and establish the (daily) prayer; who spend out of what We have provided them.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Who believe in the unseen, establish prayer, and spend out of what We have provided for them,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: the pious who believe in the unseen, attend to prayer, give in charity part of what We have granted them;

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those who believe in the unseen and keep up prayer and spend out of what We have given them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: who believe in the unseen, and are steadfast in prayer, and spend out of what We have provided them with;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Who believe in the Unseen, are steadfast in prayer, and spend out of what We have provided for them;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena yuminoona bialghaybi wayuqeemoona alssalata wamimma razaqnahum yunfiqoona

4.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ وَبِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who believe in what has been revealed to you and what was revealed to those before you, and are certain of the Hereafter.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And who believe in this (Qur’an) which has been sent down upon you, O beloved Prophet, (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) and what was sent down before you; and are certain of the Hereafter.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: who believe in what has been sent down to thee and what has been sent down before thee, and have faith in the Hereafter;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And who believe in that which hath been sent down unto thee and that which hath been sent down before thee and of the Hereafter they are convinced.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And who believe in (the Quran and the Sunnah) which has been sent down (revealed) to you (Muhammad Peace be upon him) and in [the Taurat (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel), etc.] which were sent down before you and they believe with certainty in the Hereafter. (Resurrection, recompense of their good and bad deeds, Paradise and Hell, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And those who believe in what was revealed to you, and in what was revealed before you, and are certain of the Hereafter.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: who believe in the Book We have sent down to you (i.e. the Qur'an) and in the Books sent down before you, and firmly believe in the Hereafter.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And who have faith in what is revealed to you and in what was revealed before you, and in the Hereafter they are certain.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And who believe in that which is revealed unto thee (Muhammad) and that which was revealed before thee, and are certain of the Hereafter.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and who believe in what has been sent down to you and what was sent down before you, and are certain of the Hereafter.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Who believe in that which has been sent down to you (Prophet Muhammad) and what has been sent down before you (to Prophets Jesus and Moses) and firmly believe in the Everlasting Life.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And who believe in what has been revealed to you, [O Muhammad], and what was revealed before you, and of the Hereafter they are certain [in faith].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: who have faith in what has been revealed to you and others before you and have strong faith in the life hereafter.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And who believe in that which has been revealed to you and that which was revealed before you and they are sure of the hereafter.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: those who believe in the revelation sent down to you and in what was sent before you, and firmly believe in the life to come --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And who believe in the Revelation sent to thee, and sent before thy time, and (in their hearts) have the assurance of the Hereafter.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waallatheena yuminoona bima onzila ilayka wama onzila min qablika wabialakhirati hum yooqinoona

5.arabic: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عَلَىٰ هُدًى مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They have found the guidance of their Lord and will be successful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is they who are on guidance from their Lord; and they are the successful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: those are upon guidance from their Lord, those are the ones who prosper.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: These are on guidance from their Lord, and these they are the blissful ones.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They are on (true) guidance from their Lord, and they are the successful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: These are upon guidance from their Lord. These are the successful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Such people are on the right way from their Lord and such are truly successful.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They are on guidance from their Lord, and they are the successful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: These depend on guidance from their Lord. These are the successful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Those follow their Lord’s guidance and it is they who are the felicitous.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: These are guided by their Lord; these surely are the prosperous.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those are upon [right] guidance from their Lord, and it is those who are the successful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It is the pious who follow the guidance of their Lord and gain lasting happiness.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: These are on a right course from their Lord and these it is that shall be successful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: they are the people who are rightly following their Lord and it is they who shall be successful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They are on (true) guidance, from their Lord, and it is these who will prosper.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Olaika AAala hudan min rabbihim waolaika humu almuflihoona

6.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَأَنذَرْتَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تُنذِرْهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As for those who deny, it is all the same if you warn them or not, they will not believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: As for those whose fate is disbelief, whether you warn them or do not warn them – it is all one for them; they will not believe. (Because their hearts are sealed – see next verse).

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: As for the unbelievers, alike it is to them whether thou hast warned them or hast not warned them, they do not believe.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who have disbelieved, it is equal to them whether thou warnest them or warnest them not; they will not believe.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who disbelieve, it is the same to them whether you (O Muhammad Peace be upon him) warn them or do not warn them, they will not believe.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As for those who disbelieve—it is the same for them, whether you have warned them, or have not warned them—they do not believe.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for those who have rejected (these things), it is all the same to them whether you warn them or do not warn them: they are not going to believe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who disbelieve, it is the same to them whether you warn them or do not warn them, they will not believe.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: As for the Disbelievers, Whether thou warn them or thou warn them not it is all one for them; they believe not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for the faithless, it is the same to them whether you warn them or do not warn them, they will not have faith.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who disbelieve, whether you forewarn them or not, they will not believe.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who disbelieve - it is all the same for them whether you warn them or do not warn them - they will not believe.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who deny your message will not believe whether you warn them or not

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely those who disbelieve, it being alike to them whether you warn them, or do not warn them, will not believe.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: As for those who are bent on denying the truth, it makes no difference to them whether you warn them or not, they will not believe.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: As to those who reject Faith, it is the same to them whether thou warn them or do not warn them; they will not believe.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena kafaroo sawaon AAalayhim aanthartahum am lam tunthirhum la yuminoona

7.arabic: خَتَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَعَلَىٰ سَمْعِهِمْ ۖ وَعَلَىٰٓ أَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ غِشَـٰوَةٌ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: God has sealed their hearts and ears, and veiled their eyes. For them is great deprivation.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Allah has sealed their hearts and their ears, and on their eyes is a covering; and for them is a terrible punishment.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God has set a seal on their hearts and on their hearing, and on their eyes is a covering, and there awaits them a mighty chastisement.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Allah hath set a seal upon their hearts and upon their hearing, and over their sights is a covering; and unto them shall be a torment mighty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Allah has set a seal on their hearts and on their hearings, (i.e. they are closed from accepting Allah's Guidance), and on their eyes there is a covering. Theirs will be a great torment.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: God has set a seal on their hearts and on their hearing, and over their vision is a veil. They will have a severe torment.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Allah has sealed up their hearts and ears and a covering has fallen over their eyes, and they have incurred the severest punishment.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Allah has set a seal on their hearts and on their hearing, and on their eyes there is a covering. Theirs will be a great torment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Allah hath sealed their hearing and their hearts, and on their eyes there is a covering. Theirs will be an awful doom.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Allah has set a seal on their hearts and their hearing, and there is a blindfold on their sight, and there is a great punishment for them.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Allah has set a seal upon their hearts and ears; their sight is dimmed and for them is a great punishment.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Allah has set a seal upon their hearts and upon their hearing, and over their vision is a veil. And for them is a great punishment.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God has sealed their hearts and hearing and their vision is veiled; a great punishment awaits them.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Allah has set a seal upon their hearts and upon their hearing and there is a covering over their eyes, and there is a great punishment for them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God has sealed their hearts and their ears, and over their eyes there is a covering. They will have a terrible punishment.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Allah hath set a seal on their hearts and on their hearing, and on their eyes is a veil; great is the penalty they (incur).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Khatama Allahu AAala quloobihim waAAala samAAihim waAAala absarihim ghishawatun walahum AAathabun AAatheemun

8.arabic: وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَبِٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَمَا هُم بِمُؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And there are some who, though they say: "We believe in God and the Last Day," (in reality) do not believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And among the people are some* that say, “We believe in Allah and the Last Day**” whereas they are not believers! (* The hypocrites ** the Day of Resurrection)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And some men there are who say, 'We believe in God and the Last Day'; but they are not believers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And of mankind are some who say: we believe in Allah and in the Last Day: whereas they are not believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And of mankind, there are some (hypocrites) who say: "We believe in Allah and the Last Day" while in fact they believe not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Among the people are those who say, “We believe in God and in the Last Day,” but they are not believers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then there are some who say, "We believe in Allah and the Last Day", whereas they do not believe at all.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And of mankind, there are some who say: "We believe in Allah and the Last Day" while in fact they do not believe.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And of mankind are some who say: We believe in Allah and the Last Day, when they believe not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Among the people are those who say, ‘We have faith in Allah and the Last Day,’ but they have no faith.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: There are some people who say: 'We believe in Allah and the Last Day,'yet they are not believers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And of the people are some who say, "We believe in Allah and the Last Day," but they are not believers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Some people say, "We believe in God and the Day of Judgment," but they are not true believers.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And there are some people who say: We believe in Allah and the last day; and they are not at all believers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: There are some who say, "We believe in God and the Last Day," yet they are not believers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Of the people there are some who say: "We believe in Allah and the Last Day;" but they do not (really) believe.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wamina alnnasi man yaqoolu amanna biAllahi wabialyawmi alakhiri wama hum bimumineena

9.arabic: يُخَـٰدِعُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَمَا يَخْدَعُونَ إِلَّآ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They (try to) deceive God and those who believe, yet deceive none but themselves although they do not know.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They wish to deceive Allah and the believers; and in fact they deceive none except themselves and they do not have any understanding.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They would trick God and the believers, and only themselves they deceive, and they are not aware.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They would deceive Allah and those who believe, whereas they deceive not save themselves, and they perceive not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They (think to) deceive Allah and those who believe, while they only deceive themselves, and perceive (it) not!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They seek to deceive God and those who believe, but they deceive none but themselves, though they are not aware.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They thus try to deceive Allah and the Believers, but they succeed in deceiving none except themselves and they realize it not.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They try to deceive Allah and those who believe, while they only deceive themselves, and perceive (it) not!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They think to beguile Allah and those who believe, and they beguile none save themselves; but they perceive not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They seek to deceive Allah and those who have faith, yet they deceive no one but themselves, but they are not aware.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They seek to deceive Allah and those who believe, but they deceive none except themselves, though they do not sense it.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They [think to] deceive Allah and those who believe, but they deceive not except themselves and perceive [it] not.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They deceive God and the believers. However, they have deceived no one but themselves, a fact of which they are not aware.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They desire to deceive Allah and those who believe, and they deceive only themselves and they do not perceive.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They seek to deceive God and the believers, but they only deceive themselves, though they do not realize it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Fain would they deceive Allah and those who believe, but they only deceive themselves, and realise (it) not!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: YukhadiAAoona Allaha waallatheena amanoo wama yakhdaAAoona illa anfusahum wama yashAAuroona

10.arabic: فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ فَزَادَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مَرَضًا ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْذِبُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Sick are their hearts, and God adds to their malady. For them is suffering for they lie.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In their hearts is a disease, so Allah has increased their disease; and for them is a painful punishment, because of their lies.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: In their hearts is a sickness, and God has increased their sickness, and there awaits them a painful chastisement for that they have cried lies.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: In their hearts is a disease, so Allah hath increased unto them that disease; and unto them shall be a torment afflictive, for they have been lying.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: In their hearts is a disease (of doubt and hypocrisy) and Allah has increased their disease. A painful torment is theirs because they used to tell lies.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: In their hearts is sickness, and God has increased their sickness. They will have a painful punishment because of their denial.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: In their hearts is a disease which Allah has increased all the more and a painful doom is in store for them for the lie they utter.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: In their hearts is a disease and Allah has increased their disease. A painful torment is theirs because they used to tell lies.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: In their hearts is a disease, and Allah increaseth their disease. A painful doom is theirs because they lie.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: There is a sickness in their hearts; then Allah increased their sickness, and there is a painful punishment for them because of the lies they used to tell.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: There is a sickness in their hearts which Allah has increased. For them there is a painful punishment because they lie.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: In their hearts is disease, so Allah has increased their disease; and for them is a painful punishment because they [habitually] used to lie.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: A sickness exists in their hearts to which God adds more sickness. Besides this, they will suffer a painful punishment as a result of the lie which they speak.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: There is a disease in their hearts, so Allah added to their disease and they shall have a painful chastisement because they lied.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: In their hearts is a disease, which God has increased. They will have a painful punishment, because they have been lying.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: In their hearts is a disease; and Allah has increased their disease: And grievous is the penalty they (incur), because they are false (to themselves).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fee quloobihim maradun fazadahumu Allahu maradan walahum AAathabun aleemun bima kanoo yakthiboona

11.arabic: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَا تُفْسِدُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُصْلِحُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When asked to desist from spreading corruption in the land they say: "Why, we are reformers."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when it is said to them, “Do not cause turmoil in the earth”, they say, “We are only peacemakers!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When it is said to them, 'Do not corruption in the land', they say, 'We are only ones that put things right.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when it said unto them: act not corruptly on the earth, they say: we are but reconcilers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when it is said to them: "Make not mischief on the earth," they say: "We are only peacemakers."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when it is said to them, “Do not make trouble on earth,” they say, “We are only reformers.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Whenever it is said to them, "Spread not disorder on the earth", their reply is, "We only seek to put things aright".

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when it is said to them: "Do not make mischief on the earth," they say: "We are only peacemakers."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when it is said unto them: Make not mischief in the earth, they say: We are peacemakers only.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When they are told, ‘Do not cause corruption on the earth,’ they say, ‘We are only reformers!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When it is said to them, 'Do not corrupt in the land,' they reply, 'We are only reformers.'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when it is said to them, "Do not cause corruption on the earth," they say, "We are but reformers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When they are told not to commit corruption in the land, they reply, "We are only reformers".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when it is said to them, Do not make mischief in the land, they say: We are but peace-makers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When they are told, "Do not cause corruption in the land," they say, "We are only promoters of peace,"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When it is said to them: "Make not mischief on the earth," they say: "Why, we only Want to make peace!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waitha qeela lahum la tufsidoo fee alardi qaloo innama nahnu muslihoona

12.arabic: أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْسِدُونَ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا يَشْعُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet they are surely mischief-mongers, even though they do not know.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Pay heed! It is they who are mischievous, but they do not have sense.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Truly, they are the workers of corruption but they are not aware.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Lo! verily it is they who are the corrupters, and yet they perceive not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! They are the ones who make mischief, but they perceive not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: In fact, they are the troublemakers, but they are not aware.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Beware! they do spread disorder but they realize it not.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, they are the ones who make mischief, but they perceive not.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Are not they indeed the mischief-makers? But they perceive not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Behold! They are themselves the agents of corruption, but they are not aware.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But it is they who are the evildoers, though they do not sense it.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Unquestionably, it is they who are the corrupters, but they perceive [it] not.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They, certainly, are corrupt but do not realize it.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Now surely they themselves are the mischief makers, but they do not perceive.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: but it is they who are really causing corruption, though they do not realize it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Of a surety, they are the ones who make mischief, but they realise (it) not.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ala innahum humu almufsidoona walakin la yashAAuroona

13.arabic: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ ءَامِنُوا۟ كَمَآ ءَامَنَ ٱلنَّاسُ قَالُوٓا۟ أَنُؤْمِنُ كَمَآ ءَامَنَ ٱلسُّفَهَآءُ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلسُّفَهَآءُ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا يَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When asked to believe as others do, they say: "Should we believe like fools?" And yet they are the fools, even though they do not know.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when it is said to them, “Believe as the others believe”, they say, “Shall we believe as the foolish believe?” It is they who are the fools, but they do not know.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When it is said to them, 'Believe as the people believe', they say, 'Shall we believe, as fools believe?' Truly, they are the foolish ones, but they do not know.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when it said unto them: believe even as mankind have believed, they say: shall we believe even as the fools have believed? Lo! verily it is they who are the fools; and yet they know not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when it is said to them (hypocrites): "Believe as the people (followers of Muhammad Peace be upon him, Al-Ansar and Al-Muhajirun) have believed," they say: "Shall we believe as the fools have believed?" Verily, they are the fools, but they know not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when it is said to them, “Believe as the people have believed,” they say, “Shall we believe as the fools have believed?” In fact, it is they who are the fools, but they do not know.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And when it is said to them, "Believe sincerely as the other people have believed", they reply, "Should we believe as fools have believed?" Beware! they themselves are the fools, but they know it not.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when it is said to them: Believe as the people believe," They say: "Shall we believe as the fools have believed" Verily, they are the fools, but they do not know.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when it is said unto them: believe as the people believe, they say: shall we believe as the foolish believe? are not they indeed the foolish? But they know not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when they are told, ‘Believe like the people who have believed,’ they say, ‘Shall we believe like the fools who have believed?’ Behold! They are themselves the fools, but they do not know.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When it is said to them: 'Believe as (other) people believe,' they reply, 'Are we to believe as fools believe?' It is they who are the fools, if only they knew!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when it is said to them, "Believe as the people have believed," they say, "Should we believe as the foolish have believed?" Unquestionably, it is they who are the foolish, but they know [it] not.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When they are told to believe as everyone else does, they say, "Should we believe as fools do?" In fact, they themselves are fools, but they do not know it.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when it is said to them: Believe as the people believe, they say: Shall we believe as the fools believe? Now surely they themselves are the fools, but they do not know.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: And when they are told, "Believe as other people have believed," they say, "Are we to believe just as fools believe?" Surely they are the fools, even though they do not realize it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When it is said to them: "Believe as the others believe:" They say: "Shall we believe as the fools believe?" Nay, of a surety they are the fools, but they do not know.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waitha qeela lahum aminoo kama amana alnnasu qaloo anuminu kama amana alssufahao ala innahum humu alssufahao walakin la yaAAlamoona

14.arabic: وَإِذَا لَقُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ شَيَـٰطِينِهِمْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُسْتَهْزِءُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When they meet the faithful they say: "We believe;" but when alone with the devils (their fellows), they say: "We are really with you; we were joking."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when they meet with the believers, they say, “We believe”; and when they are alone with their devils, they say, “We are undoubtedly with you, we were just mocking!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When they meet those who believe, they say, 'We believe'; but when they go privily to their Satans, they say, 'We are with you; we were only mocking.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when they meet those who have believed, they say: We believe. And when they are alone with their Satans, they say: verily we are with you, we were but mocking.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when they meet those who believe, they say: "We believe," but when they are alone with their Shayatin (devils - polytheists, hypocrites, etc.), they say: "Truly, we are with you; verily, we were but mocking."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when they come across those who believe, they say, “We believe”; but when they are alone with their devils, they say, “We are with you; we were only ridiculing.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When they meet those who believe, they say, "We, too, are believers", but when they privily meet their evil geniuses, they say, "Indeed we are with you: we are only mocking at these people".

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when they meet those who believe, they say: "We believe," but when they are alone with their Shayatin (devils), they say: "Truly, we are with you; verily, we were but mocking."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when they fall in with those who believe, they say: We believe; but when they go apart to their devils they declare: Lo! we are with you; verily we did but mock.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When they meet the faithful, they say, ‘We believe,’ but when they are alone with their devils, they say, ‘We are with you; we were only deriding [them].’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When they meet those who believe, they say, 'We, too believe.' But when they are alone with their devils, they say to them: 'We follow none but you, we were only mocking

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when they meet those who believe, they say, "We believe"; but when they are alone with their evil ones, they say, "Indeed, we are with you; we were only mockers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: To the believers they declare belief and, in secret to their own devils, they say, "We were only mocking".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when they meet those who believe, they say: We believe; and when they are alone with their Shaitans, they say: Surely we are with you, we were only mocking.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When they meet those who believe, they say, "We believe." But when they are alone with their evil leaders, they say, "We are really with you; we were only mocking."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When they meet those who believe, they say: "We believe;" but when they are alone with their evil ones, they say: "We are really with you: We (were) only jesting."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waitha laqoo allatheena amanoo qaloo amanna waitha khalaw ila shayateenihim qaloo inna maAAakum innama nahnu mustahzioona

15.arabic: ٱللَّهُ يَسْتَهْزِئُ بِهِمْ وَيَمُدُّهُمْ فِى طُغْيَـٰنِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But God will turn the joke against them and allow them to sink deeper into evil and wander perplexed in their wickedness.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Allah (befitting His Majesty) mocks them, leaving them to wander blindly in their rebellion.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: God shall mock them, and shall lead them on blindly wandering in their insolence.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Allah mocketh back at them, and letteth them in their exorbitance wander bewildered.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Allah mocks at them and gives them increase in their wrong-doings to wander blindly.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: It is God who ridicules them, and leaves them bewildered in their transgression.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (Little do they realize that) Allah is mocking at them. He gives them rope enough, and they wander on and on blindly in their mischief and rebellion.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Allah mocks at them and leaves them increasing in their deviation to wander blindly.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Allah (Himself) doth mock them, leaving them to wander blindly on in their contumacy.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: It is Allah who derides them, and leaves them bewildered in their rebellion.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Allah will mock at them and prolong them in sin, blundering blindly.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [But] Allah mocks them and prolongs them in their transgression [while] they wander blindly.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: God mocks them and gives them time to continue blindly in their transgressions.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Allah shall pay them back their mockery, and He leaves them alone in their inordinacy, blindly wandering on.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God will requite them for their mockery, and draw them on, for a while, to wander blindly in their insolence.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Allah will throw back their mockery on them, and give them rope in their trespasses; so they will wander like blind ones (To and fro).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allahu yastahzio bihim wayamudduhum fee tughyanihim yaAAmahoona

16.arabic: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱشْتَرَوُا۟ ٱلضَّلَـٰلَةَ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ فَمَا رَبِحَت تِّجَـٰرَتُهُمْ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُهْتَدِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They are indeed those who bartered away good guidance for error and gained nothing from the deal, nor found the right way.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: These are the people who purchased error in exchange of guidance – so their bargain did not profit them, and they did not know how to trade.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those are they that have bought error at the price of guidance, and their commerce has not profited them, and they are not right-guided.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: These are they who purchased error for guidance, but their traffic profited not, nor have they ever become guided.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: These are they who have purchased error for guidance, so their commerce was profitless. And they were not guided.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those are they who have bartered error for guidance; but their trade does not profit them, and they are not guided.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: These are the people who have bartered away Guidance for error, but this is a profitless bargain that they have made, and they are not at all on the right way.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: These are they who have purchased error with guidance, so their commerce was profitless. And they were not guided.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: These are they who purchase error at the price of guidance, so their commerce doth not prosper, neither are they guided.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They are the ones who bought error for guidance, so their trade did not profit them, nor were they guided.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Such are those who barter away guidance for error, their trade did not profit (them), nor are they guided.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those are the ones who have purchased error [in exchange] for guidance, so their transaction has brought no profit, nor were they guided.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They have traded guidance for error, but their bargain has had no profit and they have missed the true guidance.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: These are they who buy error for the right direction, so their bargain shall bring no gain, nor are they the followers of the right direction.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Such are those who have taken misguidance in exchange for guidance; but their bargain does not profit them, nor have they found true guidance.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: These are they who have bartered Guidance for error: But their traffic is profitless, and they have lost true direction,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Olaika allatheena ishtarawoo alddalalata bialhuda fama rabihat tijaratuhum wama kanoo muhtadeena

17.arabic: مَثَلُهُمْ كَمَثَلِ ٱلَّذِى ٱسْتَوْقَدَ نَارًا فَلَمَّآ أَضَآءَتْ مَا حَوْلَهُۥ ذَهَبَ ٱللَّهُ بِنُورِهِمْ وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتٍ لَّا يُبْصِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They are like a man who kindles a fire, and when its glow has illumined the air God takes away their light leaving them in the dark where they will not be able to see.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Their example is like that of one who kindled a fire; and when it lit up all that was around it, Allah took away their light and left them in darkness, unable to see anything.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: The likeness of them is as the likeness of a man who kindled a fire, and when it lit all about him God took away their light, and left them in darkness unseeing

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Their likeness is as the likeness of him who kindleth a fire, then when it hath lit up that which is around him, Allah taketh away their light and leaveth them in darknesses where they see not

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Their likeness is as the likeness of one who kindled a fire; then, when it lighted all around him, Allah took away their light and left them in darkness. (So) they could not see.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Their likeness is that of a person who kindled a fire; when it illuminated all around him, God took away their light, and left them in darkness, unable to see.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Their condition may be described in a parable: a man kindled a fire and when it illuminated all around him, Allah took away the light from their eyes and left them in utter darkness, where they could not see anything.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Their likeness is as the likeness of one who kindled a fire; then, when it illuminated all around him, Allah removed their light and left them in darkness. (So) they could not see.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Their likeness is as the likeness of one who kindleth fire, and when it sheddeth its light around him Allah taketh away their light and leaveth them in darkness, where they cannot see,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Their parable is that of one who lighted a torch, and when it had lit up all around him, Allah took away their light, and left them sightless in a manifold darkness.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Their example is like one who kindled a fire, and when it lit all around him, Allah took away their light and left them in darkness. They could not see.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Their example is that of one who kindled a fire, but when it illuminated what was around him, Allah took away their light and left them in darkness [so] they could not see.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Their case) is like that of one who kindles a fire and when it grows bright God takes away its light leaving him in darkness (wherein) he cannot see (anything).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Their parable is like the parable of one who kindled a fire, but when it had illumined all around him, Allah took away their light, and left them in utter darkness-- they do not see.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They are like those who kindled a fire, and when it lighted up all around them, God took away their sight and left them in utter darkness, unable to see --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Their similitude is that of a man who kindled a fire; when it lighted all around him, Allah took away their light and left them in utter darkness. So they could not see.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Mathaluhum kamathali allathee istawqada naran falamma adaat ma hawlahu thahaba Allahu binoorihim watarakahum fee thulumatin la yubsiroona

18.arabic: صُمٌّۢ بُكْمٌ عُمْىٌ فَهُمْ لَا يَرْجِعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They are deaf, dumb and blind, and shall never return;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Deaf, dumb and blind; and they are not to return.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: deaf, dumb, blind -- so they shall not return;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Deaf, dumb, blind; wherefore they will not return.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They are deaf, dumb, and blind, so they return not (to the Right Path).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Deaf, dumb, blind. They will not return.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They are deaf; they are dumb; they are blind so they will not return (to the right way).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They are deaf, dumb, and blind, so they return not (to the right path).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Deaf, dumb and blind; and they return not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Deaf, dumb, and blind, they will not come back.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Deaf, dumb, and blind, they shall never return.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Deaf, dumb and blind - so they will not return [to the right path].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They are deaf, blind, and dumb and cannot regain their senses.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Deaf, dumb (and) blind, so they will not turn back.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: deaf, dumb, and blind: they will never return [to the right path].

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Deaf, dumb, and blind, they will not return (to the path).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Summun bukmun AAumyun fahum la yarjiAAoona

19.arabic: أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فِيهِ ظُلُمَـٰتٌ وَرَعْدٌ وَبَرْقٌ يَجْعَلُونَ أَصَـٰبِعَهُمْ فِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِم مِّنَ ٱلصَّوَٰعِقِ حَذَرَ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ مُحِيطٌۢ بِٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or like rain pouring from the sky which hides within it darkness, thunder and lightning. They thrust their fingers into their ears for safety against noise and death. But God surrounds those who believe not from all sides.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or like a rainstorm from the sky, in which are darkness, thunder and lightning; they thrust their fingers in their ears due to the thunderclaps, fearing death; and Allah has the disbelievers encompassed.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: or as a cloudburst out of heaven in which is darkness, and thunder, and lightning -- they put their fingers in their ears against the thunderclaps, fearful of death; and God encompasses the unbelievers;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: like unto a rain--laden cloud from heaven, wherein are darknesses and thunder and lightening. They so put their fingers in their ears because of the thunder-claps, guarding against death, and Allah is Encompasser of the infidels.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or like a rainstorm from the sky, wherein is darkness, thunder, and lightning. They thrust their fingers in their ears to keep out the stunning thunderclap for fear of death. But Allah ever encompasses the disbelievers (i.e. Allah will gather them all together).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or like a cloudburst from the sky, in which is darkness, and thunder, and lightning. They press their fingers into their ears from the thunderbolts, in fear of death. But God surrounds the disbelievers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or (still another parable may be cited to depict their condition): heavy rain is falling from the sky, accompanied by pitch darkness, thunder and lightning. When they hear the thunderclap, they thrust their fingers into their ears for fear of death, but Allah is encircling the disbelievers on all sides.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or like a rainstorm in the sky, bringing darkness, thunder, and lightning. They thrust their fingers in their ears to keep out the stunning thunderclap for fear of death. But Allah ever encompasses the disbelievers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or like a rainstorm from the sky, wherein is darkness, thunder and the flash of lightning. They thrust their fingers in their ears by reason of the thunder-claps, for fear of death, Allah encompasseth the disbelievers (in His guidance, His omniscience and His omnipotence).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Or that of a rainstorm from the sky, wherein is darkness, thunder and lightning: they put their fingers in their ears due to the thunderclaps, apprehensive of death; and Allah besieges the faithless.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, like (those who, under) a cloudburst from the sky with darkness, thunder and lightning, they thrust their fingers in their ears at the sound of every thunderclap for fear of death, and Allah encompasses the unbelievers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or [it is] like a rainstorm from the sky within which is darkness, thunder and lightning. They put their fingers in their ears against the thunderclaps in dread of death. But Allah is encompassing of the disbelievers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Or it is like that of a rain storm with darkness, thunder, and lightning approaching. They cover their ears for fear of thunder and death. God encompasses those who deny His words.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or like abundant rain from the cloud in which is utter darkness and thunder and lightning; they put their fingers into their ears because of the thunder peal, for fear of death, and Allah encompasses the unbelievers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Or their case is like that of heavy rain pouring down from the clouds, accompanied by darkness, thunder and lightning; they thrust their fingers in their ears at the sound of thunderclaps for fear of death. God thus encompasses those who deny the truth.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or (another similitude) is that of a rain-laden cloud from the sky: In it are zones of darkness, and thunder and lightning: They press their fingers in their ears to keep out the stunning thunder-clap, the while they are in terror of death. But Allah is ever round the rejecters of Faith!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Aw kasayyibin mina alssamai feehi thulumatun waraAAdun wabarqun yajAAaloona asabiAAahum fee athanihim mina alssawaAAiqi hathara almawti waAllahu muheetun bialkafireena

20.arabic: يَكَادُ ٱلْبَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَـٰرَهُمْ ۖ كُلَّمَآ أَضَآءَ لَهُم مَّشَوْا۟ فِيهِ وَإِذَآ أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ قَامُوا۟ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Verily the lightning could snatch away their eyes. When it flashes forth they walk in its flare. When darkness returns they stand still. And if the Lord wills so He could take away their hearing and sight; surely God is all-powerful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It seems the lightning may snatch away their sight from them; whenever it flashes they walk in it, and when it darkens they stand still; if Allah willed, He could take away their hearing and their sight; indeed Allah is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: the lightning wellnigh snatches away their sight; whensoever it gives them light, they walk in it, and when the darkness is over them, they halt; had God willed, He would have taken away their hearing and their sight. Truly, God is powerful over everything.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The lightning well-nigh snatcheth away their sight; whensoever it flasheth on them, they walk therein, and when it becometh dark unto them, they stand still. And had Allah willed He would of a surety have taken away their hearing and their sights; verily Allah is over everything potent.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The lightning almost snatches away their sight, whenever it flashes for them, they walk therein, and when darkness covers them, they stand still. And if Allah willed, He could have taken away their hearing and their sight. Certainly, Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The lightning almost snatches their sight away. Whenever it illuminates for them, they walk in it; but when it grows dark over them, they stand still. Had God willed, He could have taken away their hearing and their sight. God is capable of everything.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The lightning terrifies them as if it were going to snatch away their eyesight from them. When they see light, they move on a little further and When it becomes dark for them, they stand still. Had Allah so willed, He could have deprived them totally of their hearing and their sight. Most surely Allah has power over everything.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The lightning almost snatches away their sight, whenever it flashes for them, they walk therein, and when darkness covers them, they stand still. And if Allah willed, He could have taken away their hearing and their sight. Certainly, Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The lightning almost snatcheth away their sight from them. As often as it flasheth forth for them they walk therein, and when it darkeneth against them they stand still. If Allah willed, He could destroy their hearing and their sight. Lo! Allah is able to do all things.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The lightning almost snatches away their sight: whenever it shines for them, they walk in it, and when the darkness falls upon them, they stand. Had Allah willed, He would have taken away their hearing and sight. Indeed Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The lightning almost snatches away their sight, whenever it flashes upon them they walk on, but as soon as it darkens they stand still. Indeed, if Allah willed, He could take away their sight and hearing. Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The lightning almost snatches away their sight. Every time it lights [the way] for them, they walk therein; but when darkness comes over them, they stand [still]. And if Allah had willed, He could have taken away their hearing and their sight. Indeed, Allah is over all things competent.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The lightning almost takes away their vision. When the lightning brightens their surroundings, they walk and when it is dark, they stand still. Had God wanted, He could have taken away their hearing and their vision. God has power over all things.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The lightning almost takes away their sight; whenever it shines on them they walk in it, and when it becomes dark to them they stand still; and if Allah had pleased He would certainly have taken away their hearing and their sight; surely Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The lightning almost takes away their sight: whenever it flashes upon them, they walk on, but as soon as darkness falls around them, they stand still. Indeed, if God pleased, He could take away their hearing and their sight: God has power over all things.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The lightning all but snatches away their sight; every time the light (Helps) them, they walk therein, and when the darkness grows on them, they stand still. And if Allah willed, He could take away their faculty of hearing and seeing; for Allah hath power over all things.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Yakadu albarqu yakhtafu absarahum kullama adaa lahum mashaw feehi waitha athlama AAalayhim qamoo walaw shaa Allahu lathahaba bisamAAihim waabsarihim inna Allaha AAala kulli shayin qadeerun

21.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُمْ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So, O you people, adore your Lord who created you, as He did those before you, that you could take heed for yourselves and fear Him

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O mankind! Worship your Lord, Who has created you and those before you, in the hope of attaining piety.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O you men, serve your Lord Who created you, and those that were before you; haply so you will be godfearing;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Mankind worship your Lord who hath created you and those before you, haply ye may become God-fearing-

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O mankind! Worship your Lord (Allah), Who created you and those who were before you so that you may become Al-Muttaqun (the pious - see V. 2:2).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O people! Worship your Lord who created you and those before you, that you may attain piety.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: O Mankind, submit to your Lord Who created you and those who were before you; in this way only you may expect to save yourselves.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O mankind! Worship your Lord (Allah), Who created you and those who were before you so that you may acquire Taqwa.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O mankind! worship your Lord, Who hath created you and those before you, so that ye may ward off (evil).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O mankind! Worship your Lord, who created you and those who were before you, so that you may be Godwary

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: O people, worship your Lord who has created you and those who have gone before you, so that you will be cautious.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O mankind, worship your Lord, who created you and those before you, that you may become righteous -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: People, worship your Lord who created you and those who lived before you, so that you may become pious.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O men! serve your Lord Who created you and those before you so that you may guard (against evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: People, worship your Lord, who created you and those before you, so that you may become righteous,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye people! Adore your Guardian-Lord, who created you and those who came before you, that ye may have the chance to learn righteousness;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha alnnasu oAAbudoo rabbakumu allathee khalaqakum waallatheena min qablikum laAAallakum tattaqoona

22.arabic: ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ فِرَٰشًا وَٱلسَّمَآءَ بِنَآءً وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَخْرَجَ بِهِۦ مِنَ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ رِزْقًا لَّكُمْ ۖ فَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ لِلَّهِ أَندَادًا وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who made the earth a bed for you, the sky a canopy, and sends forth rain from the skies that fruits may grow -- your food and sustenance. So, do not make another the equal of God knowingly.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The One Who has appointed the earth a base for you, and the sky a canopy – and caused water to pour down from the sky, thereby producing fruits as food for you; and do not knowingly set up rivals to Allah!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: who assigned to you the earth for a couch, and heaven for an edifice, and sent down out of heaven water, wherewith He brought forth fruits for your provision; so set not up compeers to God wittingly.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Who hath made the earth for you a carpet and the heaven a structure, and sent down from heaven water, and brought forth therewith fruits as a Provision for you; wherefore set not up compeers unto Allah while ye know.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Who has made the earth a resting place for you, and the sky as a canopy, and sent down water (rain) from the sky and brought forth therewith fruits as a provision for you. Then do not set up rivals unto Allah (in worship) while you know (that He Alone has the right to be worshipped).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He who made the earth a habitat for you, and the sky a structure, and sends water down from the sky, and brings out fruits thereby, as a sustenance for you. Therefore, do not assign rivals to God while you know.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: It is He Who has made the earth a bed for you and the sky a canopy; and it is He Who sends down rain from above for the growth of every kind of food products for your sustenance. So, when you know this, you should not set up equals to rank with Allah.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Who has made the earth a resting place for you, and the sky as a canopy, and sent down water (rain) from the sky and brought forth therewith fruits as a provision for you. Then do not set up rivals unto Allah (in worship) while you know (that He alone has the right to be worshipped).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Who hath appointed the earth a resting-place for you, and the sky a canopy; and causeth water to pour down from the sky, thereby producing fruits as food for you. And do not set up rivals to Allah when ye know (better).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —He who made the earth a place of repose for you, and the sky a canopy, and He sends down water from the sky and with it brings forth crops for your sustenance. So do not set up equals to Allah, while you know.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Who has made the earth a bed for you and the sky a dome, and has sent down water from the sky to bring forth fruits for your provision. Do not knowingly set up rivals to Allah.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [He] who made for you the earth a bed [spread out] and the sky a ceiling and sent down from the sky, rain and brought forth thereby fruits as provision for you. So do not attribute to Allah equals while you know [that there is nothing similar to Him].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Worship God who has rendered the earth as a floor for you and the sky as a dome for you and has sent water down from the sky to produce fruits for your sustenance. Do not knowingly set up anything as an equal to God.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Who made the earth a resting place for you and the heaven a canopy and (Who) sends down rain from the cloud, then brings forth with it subsistence for you of the fruits; therefore do not set up rivals to Allah while you know.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: who made the earth a bed, and the sky a canopy; and it is He who sends down rain from above for the growth of every kind of food for your sustenance. And do not knowingly set up rivals to God.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Who has made the earth your couch, and the heavens your canopy; and sent down rain from the heavens; and brought forth therewith Fruits for your sustenance; then set not up rivals unto Allah when ye know (the truth).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allathee jaAAala lakumu alarda firashan waalssamaa binaan waanzala mina alssamai maan faakhraja bihi mina alththamarati rizqan lakum fala tajAAaloo lillahi andadan waantum taAAlamoona

23.arabic: وَإِن كُنتُمْ فِى رَيْبٍ مِّمَّا نَزَّلْنَا عَلَىٰ عَبْدِنَا فَأْتُوا۟ بِسُورَةٍ مِّن مِّثْلِهِۦ وَٱدْعُوا۟ شُهَدَآءَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If you are in doubt of what We have revealed to Our votary, then bring a Surah like this, and call any witness, apart from God, you like, if you are truthful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if you are in any doubt concerning what We have sent down upon Our distinguished bondman (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), bring forth a single surah (chapter) equal to it; and call upon all your supporters, other than Allah, if you are truthful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And if you are in doubt concerning that We have sent down on Our servant, then bring a sura like it, and call your witnesses, apart from God, if you are truthful.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And if ye be in doubt concerning that which We have revealed unto Our bondsman, then bring a chapter like thereunto, and call your witnesses as against Allah, if ye say sooth.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if you (Arab pagans, Jews, and Christians) are in doubt concerning that which We have sent down (i.e. the Quran) to Our slave (Muhammad Peace be upon him), then produce a Surah (chapter) of the like thereof and call your witnesses (supporters and helpers) besides Allah, if you are truthful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And if you are in doubt about what We have revealed to Our servant, then produce a chapter like these, and call your witnesses apart from God, if you are truthful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And if you be in doubt whether the Book We have sent down to Our Servant is from Us or not, then produce, at least, one Surah like this. You may call all your associates to assist you and avail yourselves of the help of any one other than Allah. If you are genuine in your doubt, do this.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if you (Arab pagans, Jews, and Christians) are in doubt concerning that which We have sent down (i.e. the Qur'an) to Our servant (Muhammad), then produce a Surah (chapter) of the like thereof and call your witnesses (supporters and helpers) besides Allah, if you are truthful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if ye are in doubt concerning that which We reveal unto Our slave (Muhammad), then produce a surah of the like thereof, and call your witness beside Allah if ye are truthful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And if you are in doubt concerning what We have sent down to Our servant, then bring a surah like it, and invoke your helpers besides Allah, if you are truthful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If you are in doubt of what We have sent down to Our worshiper (Prophet Muhammad), produce a chapter comparable to it. Call upon your helpers, other than Allah, to assist you, if you are true.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if you are in doubt about what We have sent down upon Our Servant [Muhammad], then produce a surah the like thereof and call upon your witnesses other than Allah, if you should be truthful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Should you have any doubt about what We have revealed to Our servant, present one chapter comparable to it and call all your supporters, besides God, if your claim is true.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if you are in doubt as to that which We have revealed to Our servant, then produce a chapter like it and call on your witnesses besides Allah if you are truthful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If you are in doubt about the revelation We have sent down to Our servant, then produce a single chapter like it, and call upon your helpers besides God, if you are truthful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And if ye are in doubt as to what We have revealed from time to time to Our servant, then produce a Sura like thereunto; and call your witnesses or helpers (If there are any) besides Allah, if your (doubts) are true.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wain kuntum fee raybin mimma nazzalna AAala AAabdina fatoo bisooratin min mithlihi waodAAoo shuhadaakum min dooni Allahi in kuntum sadiqeena

24.arabic: فَإِن لَّمْ تَفْعَلُوا۟ وَلَن تَفْعَلُوا۟ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱلنَّارَ ٱلَّتِى وَقُودُهَا ٱلنَّاسُ وَٱلْحِجَارَةُ ۖ أُعِدَّتْ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But if you cannot, as indeed you cannot, then guard yourselves against the Fire whose fuel is men and rocks, which has been prepared for the infidels.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And if you are unable to bring forth (one chapter) – and We declare that you can never bring one – then fear the fire (of hell), the fuel of which is men and stones; kept ready for the disbelievers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And if you do not -- and you will not -- then fear the Fire, whose fuel is men and stones, prepared for unbelievers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: But if ye do it not, --and by no means ye shall, --then dread the Fire, the fuel whereof is men and stones, gotten ready for the infidels.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But if you do it not, and you can never do it, then fear the Fire (Hell) whose fuel is men and stones, prepared for the disbelievers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But if you do not—and you will not—then beware the Fire whose fuel is people and stones, prepared for the disbelievers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But if you do not do this, and you can never do this, then fear the Fire which has been prepared for the disbelievers and which shall have men and stones for fuel.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But if you do it not, and you can never do it, then fear the Fire (Hell) whose fuel is men and stones, prepared for the disbelievers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if ye do it not - and ye can never do it - then guard yourselves against the Fire prepared for disbelievers, whose fuel is of men and stones.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But if you do not—and you will not—then beware the Fire whose fuel will be humans and stones, prepared for the faithless.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But if you fail, as you are sure to fail, then guard yourselves against the Fire whose fuel is people and stones, prepared for the unbelievers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But if you do not - and you will never be able to - then fear the Fire, whose fuel is men and stones, prepared for the disbelievers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If you do not produce such a chapter and you never will then guard yourselves against the fire whose fuel will be people and stones and is prepared for those who hide the Truth.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But if you do (it) not and never shall you do (it), then be on your guard against the fire of which men and stones are the fuel; it is prepared for the unbelievers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But if you cannot do it, and you can never do it, then guard yourselves against the Fire whose fuel is men and stones, prepared for those who deny the truth.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But if ye cannot- and of a surety ye cannot- then fear the Fire whose fuel is men and stones,- which is prepared for those who reject Faith.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fain lam tafAAaloo walan tafAAaloo faittaqoo alnnara allatee waqooduha alnnasu waalhijaratu oAAiddat lilkafireena

25.arabic: وَبَشِّرِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۖ كُلَّمَا رُزِقُوا۟ مِنْهَا مِن ثَمَرَةٍ رِّزْقًا ۙ قَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى رُزِقْنَا مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَأُتُوا۟ بِهِۦ مُتَشَـٰبِهًا ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِيهَآ أَزْوَٰجٌ مُّطَهَّرَةٌ ۖ وَهُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Announce to those who believe and have done good deeds, glad tidings of gardens under which rivers flow, and where, when they eat the fruits that grow, they will say: "Indeed they are the same as we were given before," so like in semblance the food would be. And they shall have fair spouses there, and live there abidingly.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And give glad tidings to those who believe and do good deeds; that for them are Gardens beneath which rivers flow; when they are provided with a fruit of the Gardens, they will say, “This is the same food as what was given to us before” whereas it is in resemblance; and in the Gardens are pure spouses for them; and they shall abide in it forever.

translations.author: Arberry
