translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Attend to your prayers, give the zakat [prescribed alms] and bow down with those who bow down.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And be steadfast in prayer; practise regular charity; and bow down your heads with those who bow down (in worship).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waaqeemoo alssalata waatoo alzzakata wairkaAAoo maAAa alrrakiAAeena

44.arabic: أَتَأْمُرُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ بِٱلْبِرِّ وَتَنسَوْنَ أَنفُسَكُمْ وَأَنتُمْ تَتْلُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Will you enjoin good deeds on the others and forget your own selves? You also read the Scriptures, why do you then not understand?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: What! You enjoin righteousness upon people while you forget (to practise it) yourselves, whereas you read the Book? Do you not have sense?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Will you bid others to piety, and forget yourselves while you recite the Book? Do you not understand?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Command ye mankind to piety and forget yourselves, the while ye read the Book! Understand then ye not!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Enjoin you Al-Birr (piety and righteousness and each and every act of obedience to Allah) on the people and you forget (to practise it) yourselves, while you recite the Scripture [the Taurat (Torah)]! Have you then no sense?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Do you command people to virtuous conduct, and forget yourselves, even though you read the Scripture? Do you not understand?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: How is it that you enjoin others to follow the Right Way, but forget it yourselves, though you read the Scriptures? Have you no sense at all?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Enjoin you Al-Birr (piety and righteousness and every act of obedience to Allah) on the people and you forget (to practise it) yourselves, while you recite the Scripture (Tawrah)! Have you then no sense.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Enjoin ye righteousness upon mankind while ye yourselves forget (to practise it)? And ye are readers of the Scripture! Have ye then no sense?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Will you bid others to piety and forget yourselves, while you recite the Book? Do you not exercise your reason?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Would you order righteousness on others and forget it yourselves? Yet you recite the Book, have you no sense?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Do you order righteousness of the people and forget yourselves while you recite the Scripture? Then will you not reason?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Would you order people to do good deeds and forget to do them yourselves even though you read the Book? Why do you not think?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! do you enjoin men to be good and neglect your own souls while you read the Book; have you then no sense?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do you admonish others to do good and forget this yourselves? Yet you read the Book. Will you not then understand?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Do ye enjoin right conduct on the people, and forget (To practise it) yourselves, and yet ye study the Scripture? Will ye not understand?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Atamuroona alnnasa bialbirri watansawna anfusakum waantum tatloona alkitaba afala taAAqiloona

45.arabic: وَٱسْتَعِينُوا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ وَٱلصَّلَوٰةِ ۚ وَإِنَّهَا لَكَبِيرَةٌ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱلْخَـٰشِعِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Find strength in fortitude and prayer, which is heavy and exacting but for those who are humble and meek,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And seek help in patience and prayer; and truly it is hard except for those who prostrate before Me with sincerity.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Seek you help in patience and prayer, for grievous it is, save to the humble

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And seek help in patience and prayer and verily it is, hard except prayer unto the meek.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And seek help in patience and As-Salat (the prayer) and truly it is extremely heavy and hard except for Al-Khashi'un [i.e. the true believers in Allah - those who obey Allah with full submission, fear much from His Punishment, and believe in His Promise (Paradise, etc.) and in His Warnings (Hell, etc.)].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And seek help through patience and prayer. But it is difficult, except for the devout.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Seek help with the Salat and fortitude: no doubt, Salat is a hard task but not for those obedient servants,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And seek help in patience and As-Salat (the prayer) and truly, it is extremely heavy and hard except for Al-Khashi`in.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Seek help in patience and prayer; and truly it is hard save for the humble-minded,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And take recourse in patience and prayer, and it is indeed hard except for the humble

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And seek help in patience and prayer. For it is heavy, except to the humble,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And seek help through patience and prayer, and indeed, it is difficult except for the humbly submissive [to Allah]

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Help yourselves (in your affairs) with patience and prayer. It is a difficult task indeed, but not for the humble ones

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And seek assistance through patience and prayer, and most surely it is a hard thing except for the humble ones,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Seek help with patience and prayer; this is indeed an exacting discipline, but not to the humble,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nay, seek (Allah's) help with patient perseverance and prayer: It is indeed hard, except to those who bring a lowly spirit,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WaistaAAeenoo bialssabri waalssalati wainnaha lakabeeratun illa AAala alkhashiAAeena

46.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَـٰقُوا۟ رَبِّهِمْ وَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِ رَٰجِعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who are conscious that they have to meet their Lord, and to Him they have to return.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Who know that they have to meet their Lord, and that it is to Him they are to return.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: who reckon that they shall meet their Lord and that unto Him they are returning.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Who know that verily they are going to meet their Lord, and that verily unto Him they are going to return,

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (They are those) who are certain that they are going to meet their Lord, and that unto Him they are going to return.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who know that they will meet their Lord, and that to Him they will return.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: who realize that ultimately they shall meet their Lord and shall return to Him.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (They are those) who are certain that they are going to meet their Lord, and that unto Him they are going to return.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Who know that they will have to meet their Lord, and that unto Him they are returning.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —those who are certain that they will encounter their Lord and that they will return to Him.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: who reckon that they will meet their Lord and that to Him they will return.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Who are certain that they will meet their Lord and that they will return to Him.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: who are certain of their meeting with their Lord and their return to Him.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Who know that they shall meet their Lord and that they shall return to Him.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: who know for certain that they will meet their Lord, and that they will return to Him.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Who bear in mind the certainty that they are to meet their Lord, and that they are to return to Him.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena yathunnoona annahum mulaqoo rabbihim waannahum ilayhi rajiAAoona

47.arabic: يَـٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتِىَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَنِّى فَضَّلْتُكُمْ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, O children of Israel, the favours I bestowed on you, and made you exalted among the nations of the world.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O Descendants of Israel! Remember the favour of Mine, which I bestowed upon you and gave you superiority over others of your time. (by sending the Noble Messengers to your nation)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Children of Israel, remember My blessing wherewith I blessed you, and that I have preferred you above all beings;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Children of Israel: remember My favour wherewith I favoured you, and that verily I preferred you over the world.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O Children of Israel! Remember My Favour which I bestowed upon you and that I preferred you to the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns) (of your time period, in the past).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O Children of Israel! Remember My favor which I bestowed upon you, and I that favored you over all nations.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: O Children of Israel! Just recall to mind My favour that bestowed upon you, and remember that I exalted you above all the peoples of the world.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O Children of Israel! Remember My favor which I bestowed upon you and that I preferred you over the `Alamin (nations)

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O Children of Israel! Remember My favour wherewith I favoured you and how I preferred you to (all) creatures.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O Children of Israel, remember My blessing which I bestowed upon you, and that I gave you an advantage over all the nations.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Children of Israel, remember the favors I have bestowed on you, and that I have preferred you (the prophets among you) above the worlds.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O Children of Israel, remember My favor that I have bestowed upon you and that I preferred you over the worlds.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Children of Israel, recall My favors to you and the preference that I gave to you over all nations.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O children of Israel! call to mind My favor which I bestowed on you and that I made you excel the nations.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Children of Israel, remember My blessing which I have bestowed on you, and how I favoured you above all other people.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Children of Israel! call to mind the (special) favour which I bestowed upon you, and that I preferred you to all other (for My Message).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya banee israeela othkuroo niAAmatiya allatee anAAamtu AAalaykum waannee faddaltukum AAala alAAalameena

48.arabic: وَٱتَّقُوا۟ يَوْمًا لَّا تَجْزِى نَفْسٌ عَن نَّفْسٍ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يُقْبَلُ مِنْهَا شَفَـٰعَةٌ وَلَا يُؤْخَذُ مِنْهَا عَدْلٌ وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Take heed of the day when no man will be useful to man in the least, when no intercession matter nor ransom avail, nor help reach them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And fear the Day (of Resurrection) when no soul will be exchanged for another, nor will any intercession be accepted for the disbelievers, nor will they be set free in lieu of compensation nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and beware of a day when no soul for another shall give satisfaction, and no intercession shall be accepted from it, nor any counterpoise be taken, neither shall they be helped.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And fear a Day whereon not in aught shall a soul satisfy for a soul, nor shall intercession be accepted thereof, shall compensation be received therefor, nor shall they be succoured.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And fear a Day (of Judgement) when a person shall not avail another, nor will intercession be accepted from him nor will compensation be taken from him nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And beware of a Day when no soul will avail another in the least, nor will any intercession be accepted on its behalf, nor will any ransom be taken from it, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And guard yourselves against the Day when no one shall avail anyone anything; nor shall intercession be accepted from anyone; nor shall anyone be acquitted for any (amount of) ransom; nor shall the guilty ones be helped from any quarter.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And fear a Day (of Judgement) when a person shall not avail another, nor will intercession be accepted from him, nor will compensation be taken from him, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And guard yourselves against a day when no soul will in aught avail another, nor will intercession be accepted from it, nor will compensation be received from it, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Beware of the day when no soul will compensate for another, neither any intercession shall be accepted from it, nor any ransom shall be received from it, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Guard yourselves against the Day when no soul can avail a thing to another, when neither intercession nor ransom shall be accepted from it, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And fear a Day when no soul will suffice for another soul at all, nor will intercession be accepted from it, nor will compensation be taken from it, nor will they be aided.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Have fear of the day when every soul will be responsible for itself. No intercession or ransom will be accepted and no one will receive help.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And be on your guard against a day when one soul shall not avail another in the least, neither shall intercession on its behalf be accepted, nor shall any compensation be taken from it, nor shall they be helped.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Guard yourselves against the Day on which no soul shall in the least avail another, when neither intercession nor ransom shall be accepted from it nor shall help be given to it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then guard yourselves against a day when one soul shall not avail another nor shall intercession be accepted for her, nor shall compensation be taken from her, nor shall anyone be helped (from outside).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waittaqoo yawman la tajzee nafsun AAan nafsin shayan wala yuqbalu minha shafaAAatun wala yukhathu minha AAadlun wala hum yunsaroona

49.arabic: وَإِذْ نَجَّيْنَـٰكُم مِّنْ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ يُذَبِّحُونَ أَبْنَآءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِسَآءَكُمْ ۚ وَفِى ذَٰلِكُم بَلَآءٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, We saved you from the Pharaoh's people who wronged and oppressed you and slew your sons but spared your women: In this was a great favour from your Lord.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: (And remember) When We rescued you from Firaun’s people, for they were inflicting you with a dreadful torment, slaying your sons and sparing your daughters; that was a tremendous trial from your Lord (or a great reward).

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when We delivered you from the folk of Pharaoh who were visiting you with evil chastisement, slaughtering your sons, and sparing your women; and in that was a grievous trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time We delivered you from the house of Fir'awn imposing upon you evil torment, slaughtering your sons and letting your women live; and therein was a trial, from your Lord, Mighty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when We delivered you from Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) people, who were afflicting you with a horrible torment, killing your sons and sparing your women, and therein was a mighty trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall that We delivered you from the people of Pharaoh. They inflicted on you terrible persecution, killing your sons and sparing your women. Therein was a tremendous trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Recall the time when We delivered you from the slavery of Pharaoh's people. They had inflicted a dreadful torment on you: they killed your sons and let your daughters live. And in this there was a hard trial for you from your your Lord.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when We delivered you from Fir`awn's (Pharaoh) people, who were afflicting you with a horrible torment, killing your sons and sparing your women, and therein was a mighty trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And (remember) when We did deliver you from Pharaoh's folk, who were afflicting you with dreadful torment, slaying your sons and sparing your women: that was a tremendous trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: [Remember] when We delivered you from Pharaoh’s clan who inflicted a terrible torment on you, and slaughtered your sons and spared your women, and in that there was a great test from your Lord.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (Remember) how We saved you from Pharaoh's people who had oppressed you cruelly, slaying your sons and sparing your women. Surely, that was a great trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when We saved your forefathers from the people of Pharaoh, who afflicted you with the worst torment, slaughtering your [newborn] sons and keeping your females alive. And in that was a great trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Children of Israel, recall My favor) of saving you from the Pharaoh's people who afflicted you with the worst kind of cruelty, slaying your sons and sparing your women. Your suffering was indeed a great trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when We delivered you from Firon's people, who subjected you to severe torment, killing your sons and sparing your women, and in this there was a great trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Remember when We delivered you from Pharaoh's people, who subjected you to grievous torment, slaying your sons and sparing only your daughters,  surely in that there was a great trial for you from your Lord,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember, We delivered you from the people of Pharaoh: They set you hard tasks and punishments, slaughtered your sons and let your women-folk live; therein was a tremendous trial from your Lord.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith najjaynakum min ali firAAawna yasoomoonakum sooa alAAathabi yuthabbihoona abnaakum wayastahyoona nisaakum wafee thalikum balaon min rabbikum AAatheemun

50.arabic: وَإِذْ فَرَقْنَا بِكُمُ ٱلْبَحْرَ فَأَنجَيْنَـٰكُمْ وَأَغْرَقْنَآ ءَالَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, We parted the sea and saved you, and drowned the men of Pharaoh before your very eyes.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when We split the sea for you thereby rescuing you, and drowned the Firaun's people in front of your eyes.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when We divided for you the sea and delivered you, and drowned Pharaoh's folk while you were beholding.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time We separated the sea for you and delivered you and drowned Fir'awn's house while ye looked on.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when We separated the sea for you and saved you and drowned Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) people while you were looking (at them, when the sea-water covered them).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall that We parted the sea for you, so We saved you, and We drowned the people of Pharaoh as you looked on.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Remember the time when We parted the sea to make way for you and let you pass safely through it and then drowned Pharaoh's people before your very eyes.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when We separated the sea for you and saved you and drowned Fir`awn's (Pharaoh) people while you were watching.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when We brought you through the sea and rescued you, and drowned the folk of Pharaoh in your sight.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when We parted the sea with you, and We delivered you and drowned Pharaoh’s clan as you looked on.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We parted the sea for you, and, taking you to safety, drowned the family of Pharaoh before your eyes.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when We parted the sea for you and saved you and drowned the people of Pharaoh while you were looking on.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We parted the sea to save you and drowned Pharaoh's people before your very eyes.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when We parted the sea for you, so We saved you and drowned the followers of Firon and you watched by.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and when We divided the sea for you and saved you and drowned Pharaoh's people, while you looked on.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember We divided the sea for you and saved you and drowned Pharaoh's people within your very sight.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith faraqna bikumu albahra faanjaynakum waaghraqna ala firAAawna waantum tanthuroona

51.arabic: وَإِذْ وَٰعَدْنَا مُوسَىٰٓ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً ثُمَّ ٱتَّخَذْتُمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ وَأَنتُمْ ظَـٰلِمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet, remember, as We communed with Moses for forty nights you took the calf in his absence (and worshipped it), and you did wrong.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when We made a commitment with Moosa (Moses) for forty nights – then behind him you started worshipping the calf, and you were unjust.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when We appointed with Moses forty nights then you took to yourselves the Calf after him and you were evildoers;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time We treated with Musa forty nights, then ye betook the calf after him, and ye were wrong-doers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when We appointed for Musa (Moses) forty nights, and (in his absence) you took the calf (for worship), and you were Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall that We appointed for Moses forty nights. Then you took to worshiping the calf after him, and you turned wicked.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Call to mind that when We invited Moses for a fixed term of forty nights and days, you took to calf worship in his absence. Though you had committed a wicked transgression,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when We appointed for Musa (Moses) forty nights, and (in his absence) you took the calf (for worship), and you were Zalimun (polytheists and wrongdoers).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when We did appoint for Moses forty nights (of solitude), and then ye chose the calf, when he had gone from you, and were wrong-doers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when We made an appointment with Moses for forty nights, you took up the Calf [for worship] in his absence, and you were wrongdoers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We appointed for Moses forty nights, but in his absence you took the calf, thereby committing harm.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when We made an appointment with Moses for forty nights. Then you took [for worship] the calf after him, while you were wrongdoers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Then We called Moses for an appointment of forty nights. You began to worship the calf in his absence, doing wrong to yourselves.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when We appointed a time of forty nights with Musa, then you took the calf (for a god) after him and you were unjust.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We appointed for Moses forty nights [on Mount Sinai], and in his absence you took to worshipping the calf, and thus became transgressors.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember We appointed forty nights for Moses, and in his absence ye took the calf (for worship), and ye did grievous wrong.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith waAAadna moosa arbaAAeena laylatan thumma ittakhathtumu alAAijla min baAAdihi waantum thalimoona

52.arabic: ثُمَّ عَفَوْنَا عَنكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Even so, We pardoned you that you may be grateful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then after that We pardoned you so that you may be grateful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: then We pardoned you after that, that haply you should be thankful.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then We pardoned you thereafter, that haply ye may return thanks.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then after that We forgave you so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then We pardoned you after that, so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: yet We pardoned you even after that so that you might become grateful.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then after that We forgave you so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then, even after that, We pardoned you in order that ye might give thanks.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then We excused you after that so that you might give thanks.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Yet after that We pardoned you, so that you might give thanks.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then We forgave you after that so perhaps you would be grateful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Afterwards, We forgave you so that you would perhaps appreciate Our favors.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then We pardoned you after that so that you might give thanks.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Yet after that We pardoned you, so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Even then We did forgive you; there was a chance for you to be grateful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thumma AAafawna AAankum min baAAdi thalika laAAallakum tashkuroona

53.arabic: وَإِذْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْفُرْقَانَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, We gave Moses the Book and Discernment of falsehood and truth, that you may be guided.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when We gave Moosa the Book (Taurat / Torah) and the criterion to judge right from wrong, so that you may attain guidance.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when We gave to Moses the Book and the Salvation, that haply you should be guided.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time We vouchsafed unto Musa the Book and the distinction that haply ye may be guided.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when We gave Musa (Moses) the Scripture [the Taurat (Torah)] and the criterion (of right and wrong) so that you may be guided aright.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall that We gave Moses the Scripture and the Criterion, so that you may be guided.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Remember that (at that very time, when you were committing this gross iniquity) We gave Moses the Book and the criterion of right and wrong so that you might be guided aright.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when We gave Musa the Scripture (the Tawrah) and the criterion (of right and wrong) so that you may be guided aright.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when We gave unto Moses the Scripture and the criterion (of right and wrong), that ye might be led aright.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when We gave Moses the Book and the Criterion so that you might be guided.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when We gave Moses the Book and the Criterion, so that you might be guided;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when We gave Moses the Scripture and criterion that perhaps you would be guided.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We gave Moses the Book and the criteria (of discerning right from wrong) so that perhaps you would be rightly guided.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when We gave Musa the Book and the distinction that you might walk aright.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Remember when We gave Moses the Scripture, and the criterion [to distinguish between right and wrong], so that you might be guided.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember We gave Moses the Scripture and the Criterion (Between right and wrong): There was a chance for you to be guided aright.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith atayna moosa alkitaba waalfurqana laAAallakum tahtadoona

54.arabic: وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِنَّكُمْ ظَلَمْتُمْ أَنفُسَكُم بِٱتِّخَاذِكُمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ فَتُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ بَارِئِكُمْ فَٱقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ عِندَ بَارِئِكُمْ فَتَابَ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, Moses said: "My people, by taking this calf you have done yourselves harm, so now turn to your Creator in repentance, and kill your pride, which is better with your Lord." And (the Lord) softened towards you, for He is all-forgiving and merciful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when Moosa said to his people, “O my people! You have wronged yourselves by taking the calf,* therefore turn in repentance to your Creator, therefore kill each other; this is better for you before your Creator”; He therefore accepted your repentance; indeed He only is the Most Acceptor of Repentance, the Most Merciful. (* as your deity for worship)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when Moses said to his people, 'My people, you have done wrong against yourselves by your taking the Calf; now turn to your Creator and slay one another. That will be better for you in your Creator's sight, and He will turn to you; truly He turns, and is All-compassionate.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time Musa said unto his people: my people! verily ye have wronged your souls by your taking the calf, wherefore repent unto your Maker, and slay yourselves: that were best for you with your Maker. Then He relented toward you; verily He! He is the Relentant, the Merciful

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when Musa (Moses) said to his people: "O my people! Verily, you have wronged yourselves by worshipping the calf. So turn in repentance to your Creator and kill yourselves (the innocent kill the wrongdoers among you), that will be better for you with your Lord." Then He accepted your repentance. Truly, He is the One Who accepts repentance, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall that Moses said to his people, “O my people, you have done wrong to yourselves by worshiping the calf. So repent to your Maker, and kill your egos. That would be better for you with your Maker.” So He turned to you in repentance. He is the Accepter of Repentance, the Merciful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Remember that when Moses (returned with the Divine Gift, he) said to his people, "O my people, you have wronged yourselves grievously by taking the calf for worship. Therefore, turn to your Creator. in penitence and slay the guilty ones among you. This is best for you in the sight of your Creator." At that time your Creator accepted your repentance because He is Relenting 'and Merciful.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when Musa said to his people: "O my people! Verily, you have wronged yourselves by worshipping the calf. So turn in repentance to your Creator and kill yourselves (the innocent kill the wrongdoers among you), that will be better for you with your Creator." Then He accepted your repentance. Truly, He is the One Who accepts repentance, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when Moses said unto his people: O my people! Ye have wronged yourselves by your choosing of the calf (for worship) so turn in penitence to your Creator, and kill (the guilty) yourselves. That will be best for you with your Creator and He will relent toward you. Lo! He is the Relenting, the Merciful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And [recall] when Moses said to his people, ‘O my people! You have indeed wronged yourselves by taking up the Calf [for worship]. Now turn penitently to your Maker, and slay [the guilty among] your folks. That will be better for you with your Maker.’ Then He turned to you clemently. Indeed, He is the All-clement, the All-merciful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and when Moses said to his nation: 'My nation, you have harmed yourselves by taking the calf (to worship). So turn in repentance to your Creator and slay yourselves. That will be best for you with your Creator.' And He shall accept you. He is the Receiver of Repentance, the Merciful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when Moses said to his people, "O my people, indeed you have wronged yourselves by your taking of the calf [for worship]. So repent to your Creator and kill yourselves. That is best for [all of] you in the sight of your Creator." Then He accepted your repentance; indeed, He is the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Moses said to his people, "My people, you have done wrong to yourselves by worshipping the calf. Seek pardon from your Lord and slay yourselves." He told them that it would be best for them in the sight of their Lord, Who would forgive them, for He is All-forgiving and All-merciful.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when Musa said to his people: O my people! you have surely been unjust to yourselves by taking the calf (for a god), therefore turn to your Creator (penitently), so kill your people, that is best for you with your Creator: so He turned to you (mercifully), for surely He is the Oft-returning (to mercy), the Merciful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When Moses said to his people, "O my people, you have indeed wronged yourselves by worshipping the calf; turn in repentance to your Creator and slay [the culprits] among you. That course is best for you in the sight of your Maker." He relented towards you. He is the Forgiving One, the Merciful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember Moses said to his people: "O my people! Ye have indeed wronged yourselves by your worship of the calf: So turn (in repentance) to your Maker, and slay yourselves (the wrong-doers); that will be better for you in the sight of your Maker." Then He turned towards you (in forgiveness): For He is Oft-Returning, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith qala moosa liqawmihi ya qawmi innakum thalamtum anfusakum biittikhathikumu alAAijla fatooboo ila bariikum faoqtuloo anfusakum thalikum khayrun lakum AAinda bariikum fataba AAalaykum innahu huwa alttawwabu alrraheemu

55.arabic: وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ لَن نُّؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّىٰ نَرَى ٱللَّهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ ٱلصَّـٰعِقَةُ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, when you said to Moses: "We shall not believe in you until we see God face to face," lightning struck you as you looked.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when you said “O Moosa! We will not believe you till we clearly see Allah”; so the thunder seized you while you were watching.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when you said, 'Moses, we will not believe thee till we see God openly'; and the thunderbolt took you while you were beholding.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time ye said: O Musa! we will not believe in thee until we see God openly; then a thunderbolt took hold of you while ye looked on.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when you said: "O Musa (Moses)! We shall never believe in you till we see Allah plainly." But you were seized with a thunderbolt (lightning) while you were looking.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall that you said, “O Moses, we will not believe in you unless we see God plainly.” Thereupon the thunderbolt struck you, as you looked on.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Remember when you said, "O Moses, we are not going to believe you until we see with our own eyes Allah (talking to you)". At that very time a thunderbolt struck you while you were looking on and you fell lifeless.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when you said: "O Musa! We shall never believe in you until we see Allah plainly." But you were seized with a bolt of lightning while you were looking.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when ye said: O Moses! We will not believe in thee till we see Allah plainly; and even while ye gazed the lightning seized you.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when you said, ‘O Moses, we will not believe you until we see Allah visibly.’ Thereupon a thunderbolt seized you as you looked on.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when you said to Moses: 'We will not believe in you until we see Allah openly' a thunderbolt struck you whilst you were looking.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when you said, "O Moses, we will never believe you until we see Allah outright"; so the thunderbolt took you while you were looking on.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When you argued with Moses, saying that you were not going to believe him unless you could see God with your own eyes, the swift wind struck you and you could do nothing but watch.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when you said: O Musa! we will not believe in you until we see Allah manifestly, so the punishment overtook you while you looked on.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Remember when you said, "Moses, we will not believe in you until we see God with our own eyes," a thunderbolt struck you while you were looking on.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember ye said: "O Moses! We shall never believe in thee until we see Allah manifestly," but ye were dazed with thunder and lighting even as ye looked on.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith qultum ya moosa lan numina laka hatta nara Allaha jahratan faakhathatkumu alssaAAiqatu waantum tanthuroona

56.arabic: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَـٰكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَوْتِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Even then We revived you after you had become senseless that you might give thanks;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then We brought you back to life after your death, so that you may be grateful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then We raised you up after you were dead, that haply you should be thankful.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then We raised you after your death that haply ye may return thanks.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then We raised you up after your death, so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then We revived you after your death, so that you may be appreciative.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then We raised you to life so that you might become grateful for this favour.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then We raised you up after your death, so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then We revived you after your extinction, that ye might give thanks.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then We raised you up after your death so that you might give thanks.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Then We raised you from your death, so that you might give thanks.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then We revived you after your death that perhaps you would be grateful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Then We brought you back to life in the hope that you might appreciate Our favors.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then We raised you up after your death that you may give thanks.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then We brought you back to life after your death, so that you might be grateful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then We raised you up after your death: Ye had the chance to be grateful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thumma baAAathnakum min baAAdi mawtikum laAAallakum tashkuroona

57.arabic: وَظَلَّلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْغَمَامَ وَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلْوَىٰ ۖ كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَـٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَـٰكُمْ ۖ وَمَا ظَلَمُونَا وَلَـٰكِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And made the cloud spread shade over you, and sent for you manna and quails that you may eat of the good things We have made for you. No harm was done to Us, they only harmed themselves.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We made the clouds a canopy for you and sent down Manna and Salwa (birds) on you; “Eat of the pure things We have provided you”; they did not wrong Us in the least, but indeed they wronged themselves.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We outspread the cloud to overshadow you, and We sent down manna and quails upon you: 'Eat of the good things wherewith We have provided you. And they worked no wrong upon Us, but themselves they wronged.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We overshadowed you with Clouds and We sent down unto you the Manna and the quails, saying: eat of the clean things, wherewith We have provided you. And they wronged Us not, but themselves they were wont to wrong.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And We shaded you with clouds and sent down on you Al-Manna and the quails, (saying): "Eat of the good lawful things We have provided for you," (but they rebelled). And they did not wrong Us but they wronged themselves.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And We shaded you with clouds, and We sent down to you manna and quails: “Eat of the good things We have provided for you.” They did not wrong Us, but they used to wrong their own souls.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (Remember that) We caused the cloud to overshadow you and provided you with manna and salva for your food, saying, "Eat of the clean and pure things We have bestowed upon you" (In spite of this, your forefathers violated Our commands:) however, they did not harm Us but harmed only themselves.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And We shaded you with clouds and sent down on you Al-Manna and the quail, (saying): "Eat of the good lawful things We have provided for you," (but they rebelled). And they did not wrong Us but they wronged themselves.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We caused the white cloud to overshadow you and sent down on you the manna and the quails, (saying): Eat of the good things wherewith We have provided you - they wronged Us not, but they did wrong themselves.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We shaded you with clouds, and sent down to you manna and quails [saying]: ‘Eat of the good things We have provided for you.’ And they did not wrong Us, but they used to wrong [only] themselves.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We caused the clouds to cast their shadow over you and sent down for you manna and quails, (saying:) 'Eat of the good things We have provided you. 'Indeed, they did not harm Us, but they harmed themselves.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We shaded you with clouds and sent down to you manna and quails, [saying], "Eat from the good things with which We have provided you." And they wronged Us not - but they were [only] wronging themselves.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We provided you with shade from the clouds and sent down manna and quails as the best pure sustenance for you to eat. They (children of Israel) did not wrong Us but wronged themselves.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We made the clouds to give shade over you and We sent to you manna and quails: Eat of the good things that We have given you; and they did not do Us any harm, but they made their own souls suffer the loss.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then We caused the clouds to provide shade for you and sent down for you manna and quails, saying, "Eat the good things We have provided for you." Indeed, they did not wrong Us, but they wronged themselves.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And We gave you the shade of clouds and sent down to you Manna and quails, saying: "Eat of the good things We have provided for you:" (But they rebelled); to us they did no harm, but they harmed their own souls.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wathallalna AAalaykumu alghamama waanzalna AAalaykumu almanna waalssalwa kuloo min tayyibati ma razaqnakum wama thalamoona walakin kanoo anfusahum yathlimoona

58.arabic: وَإِذْ قُلْنَا ٱدْخُلُوا۟ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْقَرْيَةَ فَكُلُوا۟ مِنْهَا حَيْثُ شِئْتُمْ رَغَدًا وَٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْبَابَ سُجَّدًا وَقُولُوا۟ حِطَّةٌ نَّغْفِرْ لَكُمْ خَطَـٰيَـٰكُمْ ۚ وَسَنَزِيدُ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And remember, We said to you: "Enter this city, eat wherever you like, as much as you please, but pass through the gates in humility and say: 'May our sins be forgiven.' We shall forgive your trespasses and give those who do good abundance.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when We said, “Enter this town and eat freely from what is in it, and enter the gate whilst prostrating, and say, ‘May our sins be forgiven’ – We will forgive you your sins; and We will soon increase the reward for the righteous.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when We said, 'Enter this township, and eat easefully of it wherever you will, and enter in at the gate, prostrating, and say, Unburdening; We will forgive you your transgressions, and increase the good-doers.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time We said: enter this town and eat plenteously therefrom as ye list, and enter the gate prostrating yourselves and say: forgiveness; We shall forgive you your trespasses, and anon We shall increase unto the well-doers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when We said: "Enter this town (Jerusalem) and eat bountifully therein with pleasure and delight wherever you wish, and enter the gate in prostration (or bowing with humility) and say: 'Forgive us,' and We shall forgive you your sins and shall increase (reward) for the good-doers."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall that We said, “Enter this town, and eat plentifully from it whatever you wish; but enter the gate humbly, and say, 'Pardon.' We will forgive your sins, and give increase to the virtuous.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then call to mind the time when We said, "Go into the town' before you and eat to your hearts' content therein, wherefrom you will, but enter the gate bowing down with humility, repeating 'hittatun'; We will forgive your sins and increase the reward of the righteous".

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when We said: "Enter this town (Jerusalem) and eat bountifully therein with pleasure and delight wherever you wish, and enter the gate in prostration (or bowing with humility) and say: `Forgive us,' and We shall forgive you your sins and shall increase (reward) for the good-doers."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when We said: Go into this township and eat freely of that which is therein, and enter the gate prostrate, and say: "Repentance." We will forgive you your sins and will increase (reward) for the right-doers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when We said, ‘Enter this town, and eat thereof freely whencesoever you wish, and enter while prostrating at the gate, and say, ‘‘Relieve [us of the burden of our sins],’’ so that We may forgive your iniquities and We will soon enhance the virtuous.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Enter this village' We said, 'and eat wherever you will and as much as you wish. Make your way prostrating through the gates, saying: "Unburdening." We shall forgive you your sins and We will increase the gooddoers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when We said, "Enter this city and eat from it wherever you will in [ease and] abundance, and enter the gate bowing humbly and say, 'Relieve us of our burdens.' We will [then] forgive your sins for you, and We will increase the doers of good [in goodness and reward]."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Children of Israel, recall My favors) when We told you, "Enter this city, enjoy eating whatever you want therein, prostrate yourselves and ask forgiveness when passing through the gate, and We shall forgive your sins, and add to the rewards of the righteous ones".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when We said: Enter this city, then eat from it a plenteous (food) wherever you wish, and enter the gate making obeisance, and say, forgiveness. We will forgive you your wrongs and give more to those who do good (to others).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Remember when We said, "Enter this land and eat freely wherever you will. Make your way through the gates, bowing down and saying, "God! Forgive us our sins." We shall forgive you your sins and We shall give abundance to those who do good."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember We said: "Enter this town, and eat of the plenty therein as ye wish; but enter the gate with humility, in posture and in words, and We shall forgive you your faults and increase (the portion of) those who do good."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith qulna odkhuloo hathihi alqaryata fakuloo minha haythu shitum raghadan waodkhuloo albaba sujjadan waqooloo hittatun naghfir lakum khatayakum wasanazeedu almuhsineena

59.arabic: فَبَدَّلَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ قَوْلًا غَيْرَ ٱلَّذِى قِيلَ لَهُمْ فَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ رِجْزًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But the wicked changed and perverted the word We had spoken to a word distorted, and We sent from heaven retribution on the wicked, for they disobeyed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: But the unjust changed the word that had been ordered for another one, so We sent down a punishment on them from the skies, the recompense of their disobedience.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then the evildoers substituted a saying other than that which had been said to them; so We sent down upon the evildoers wrath out of heaven for their ungodliness.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then those who did wrong changed the Word that had been told them for anot her; so We sent down upon those who did wrong a scourge from heaven, for they were wont to transgress.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But those who did wrong changed the word from that which had been told to them for another, so We sent upon the wrong-doers Rijzan (a punishment) from the heaven because of their rebelling against Allah's Obedience. (Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. I, Page 305).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But the wrongdoers among them substituted words other than those given to them, so We sent down on the wrongdoers a plague from heaven, because of their wicked behavior.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But the transgressors perverted the words said to them entirely into a different thing. So We sent down upon the transgressors a severe torment from the sky: that was the punishment for the disobedience they were showing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But those who did wrong changed the word from that which had been told to them for another, so We sent upon the wrongdoers Rijz (a punishment) from the heaven because of their rebellion.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But those who did wrong changed the word which had been told them for another saying, and We sent down upon the evil-doers wrath from heaven for their evil-doing.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But the wrongdoers changed the saying with other than what they were told. So We sent down on those who were wrongdoers a plague from the sky because of the transgressions they used to commit.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But the harmdoers tampered with Our Words, different from that said to them, and We let loose on the harmdoers a scourge from heaven as a punishment for their debauchery.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But those who wronged changed [those words] to a statement other than that which had been said to them, so We sent down upon those who wronged a punishment from the sky because they were defiantly disobeying.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The unjust ones among you changed what they were told to say. Then, We afflicted them with a torment from the heavens for their evil deeds.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But those who were unjust changed it for a saying other than that which had been spoken to them, so We sent upon those who were unjust a pestilence from heaven, because they transgressed.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But the wrongdoers changed the words to something other than what they had been told. So We sent down upon the transgressors a punishment from heaven, because they were disobedient.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But the transgressors changed the word from that which had been given them; so We sent on the transgressors a plague from heaven, for that they infringed (Our command) repeatedly.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fabaddala allatheena thalamoo qawlan ghayra allathee qeela lahum faanzalna AAala allatheena thalamoo rijzan mina alssamai bima kanoo yafsuqoona

60.arabic: وَإِذِ ٱسْتَسْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦ فَقُلْنَا ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْحَجَرَ ۖ فَٱنفَجَرَتْ مِنْهُ ٱثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا ۖ قَدْ عَلِمَ كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ مَّشْرَبَهُمْ ۖ كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ مِن رِّزْقِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And remember, when Moses asked for water for his people, We told him to strike the rock with his staff, and behold, twelve springs of gushing water gushed forth so that each of the tribes came to know its place of drinking. Eat and drink, (enjoy) God's gifts, and spread no discord in the land.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when Moosa asked for water for his people, We said, “Strike this rock with your staff”; thereupon twelve springs gushed forth from it; each group recognised its drinking-place; “Eat and drink from what Allah has provided, and do not roam about the earth making turmoil in it.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when Moses sought water for his people, so We said, 'Strike with thy staff the rock'; and there gushed forth from it twelve fountains; all the people knew now their drinking-place. 'Eat and drink of God's providing, and mischief not in the earth, doing corruption.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time Musa prayed for drink for his people. We said: smite With thy staff the stone. Then there gushed forth thereout twelve springs; every people already knew their drinking-place; eat and drink of the provision of Allah, and commit not evil on the earth as corrupters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when Musa (Moses) asked for water for his people, We said: "Strike the stone with your stick." Then gushed forth therefrom twelve springs. Each (group of) people knew its own place for water. "Eat and drink of that which Allah has provided and do not act corruptly, making mischief on the earth."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall when Moses prayed for water for his people. We said, “Strike the rock with your staff.” Thereupon twelve springs gushed out from it, and each tribe recognized its drinking-place. “Eat and drink from God’s provision, and do not corrupt the earth with disobedience.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Remember that when Moses prayed for water for his people, We answered, "Strike the rock with your staff": whereupon twelve springs gushed forth from it; the people of every clan came to know their drinking place. (Then they were enjoined:) "Eat and drink of what Allah has provided and do not spread disorder on the earth."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when Musa asked for water for his people, We said: "Strike the stone with your stick." Then gushed forth therefrom twelve springs. Each (group of) people knew its own place for water. "Eat and drink of that which Allah has provided and do not act corruptly, making mischief on the earth."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when Moses asked for water for his people, We said: Smite with thy staff the rock. And there gushed out therefrom twelve springs (so that) each tribe knew their drinking-place. Eat and drink of that which Allah hath provided, and do not act corruptly, making mischief in the earth.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when Moses prayed for water for his people, We said, ‘Strike the rock with your staff.’ Thereat twelve fountains gushed forth from it; every tribe came to know its drinking-place. ‘Eat and drink of Allah’s provision, and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (Remember) when Moses prayed for water for his nation, We said to him: 'Strike the Rock with your staff' Thereupon twelve springs gushed from it, and each tribe knew their drinking place. 'Eat and drink of that which Allah has provided and do not act evilly in the land, corrupting'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when Moses prayed for water for his people, so We said, "Strike with your staff the stone." And there gushed forth from it twelve springs, and every people knew its watering place. "Eat and drink from the provision of Allah, and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When Moses prayed for rain, We told him to strike the rock with his staff. Thereupon twelve fountains gushed out of the rock and each tribe knew their drinking place. The Lord told them, "Eat and drink from God's bounties and do not abuse the earth with corruption."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when Musa prayed for drink for his people, We said: Strike the rock with your staff. So there gushed from it twelve springs; each tribe knew its drinking place: Eat and drink of the provisions of Allah and do not act corruptly in the land, making mischief.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When Moses prayed for water for his people, We said to him, "Strike the rock with your staff!" And there gushed out from it twelve springs. Each tribe knew its drinking-place. [We said], "Eat and drink what God has provided and do not act wrongfully in the land, spreading corruption."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember Moses prayed for water for his people; We said: "Strike the rock with thy staff." Then gushed forth therefrom twelve springs. Each group knew its own place for water. So eat and drink of the sustenance provided by Allah, and do no evil nor mischief on the (face of the) earth.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waithi istasqa moosa liqawmihi faqulna idrib biAAasaka alhajara fainfajarat minhu ithnata AAashrata AAaynan qad AAalima kullu onasin mashrabahum kuloo waishraboo min rizqi Allahi wala taAAthaw fee alardi mufsideena

61.arabic: وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ لَن نَّصْبِرَ عَلَىٰ طَعَامٍ وَٰحِدٍ فَٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُخْرِجْ لَنَا مِمَّا تُنۢبِتُ ٱلْأَرْضُ مِنۢ بَقْلِهَا وَقِثَّآئِهَا وَفُومِهَا وَعَدَسِهَا وَبَصَلِهَا ۖ قَالَ أَتَسْتَبْدِلُونَ ٱلَّذِى هُوَ أَدْنَىٰ بِٱلَّذِى هُوَ خَيْرٌ ۚ ٱهْبِطُوا۟ مِصْرًا فَإِنَّ لَكُم مَّا سَأَلْتُمْ ۗ وَضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلذِّلَّةُ وَٱلْمَسْكَنَةُ وَبَآءُو بِغَضَبٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا عَصَوا۟ وَّكَانُوا۟ يَعْتَدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, when you said: "O Moses, we are tired of eating the same food (day after day), ask your Lord to give us fruits of the earth, herbs and cucumbers, grains and lentils and onions;" he said: "Would you rather exchange what is good with what is bad? Go then to the city, you shall have what you ask." So they were disgraced and became indigent, earning the anger of God, for they disbelieved the word of God, and slayed the prophets unjustly, for they transgressed and rebelled.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when you said, “O Moosa! We shall never put up with only one kind of food, so call upon your Lord to produce for us what the earth grows – some herbs, cucumbers, corn, lentils and onions”; he said, “What! You wish to exchange the better for something inferior? Therefore settle down in Egypt or any city, where you will get what you demand”; and disgrace and misery were destined for them; and they returned towards Allah’s wrath; that was because they disbelieved in Allah’s signs and wrongfully martyred the Prophets; that was for their disobedience and transgression.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when you said, 'Moses, we will not endure one sort of food; pray to thy Lord for us, that He may bring forth for us of that the earth produces - green herbs, cucumbers, corn, lentils, onions.' He said, 'Would you have in exchange what is meaner for what is better? Get you down to Egypt; you shall have there that you demanded.' And abasement and poverty were pitched upon them, and they were laden with the burden of God's anger; that, because they had disbelieved the signs of God and slain the Prophets unrightfully; that, because they disobeyed, and were transgressors.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time ye said: O Musa! we shall by no means bear patiently with one food, wherefore supplicate for us unto thy Lord that He bring forth for us of that which the earth groweth, of its vegetables, and its cucumbers, and its Wheat, and its lentils, and its onions. He Said: would ye take in exchange that which is mean for that which is better! Get ye down into a City, as verily therein is for you that which ye ask for. And stuck upon them were abjection and poverty. And they drew on themselves indignation from Allah. This, because they were ever disbelieving in the signs of Allah and slaying the prophets without justice. This, because they disobeyed and were ever trespassing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when you said, "O Musa (Moses)! We cannot endure one kind of food. So invoke your Lord for us to bring forth for us of what the earth grows, its herbs, its cucumbers, its Fum (wheat or garlic), its lentils and its onions." He said, "Would you exchange that which is better for that which is lower? Go you down to any town and you shall find what you want!" And they were covered with humiliation and misery, and they drew on themselves the Wrath of Allah. That was because they used to disbelieve the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of Allah and killed the Prophets wrongfully. That was because they disobeyed and used to transgress the bounds (in their disobedience to Allah, i.e. commit crimes and sins).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall when you said, “O Moses, we cannot endure one kind of food, so call to your Lord to produce for us of what the earth grows: of its herbs, and its cucumbers, and its garlic, and its lentils, and its onions.” He said, “Would you substitute worse for better? Go down to Egypt, where you will have what you asked for.” They were struck with humiliation and poverty, and incurred wrath from God. That was because they rejected God’s revelations and wrongfully killed the prophets. That was because they disobeyed and transgressed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Remember: You grumbled: "O Moses, we cannot endure one and the same sort of food. Pray your Lord to bring for us the products of the earth green herbs, vegetables, corn, garlic, onions, pulses and the like." Moses replied: "What! would you exchange that which is meaner for that which is nobler? Well, go and live in a town and you will get there what you demand." By and by, they became so degraded that disgrace and humiliation, misery and wretchedness were stamped upon them and they incurred Allah's wrath. That was because they began to reject the Revelations of Allah and kill His Messengers without any just cause; that was the consequence of their disobedience and their persistent transgression against the Law.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when you said, "O Musa! We cannot endure one kind of food. So invoke your Lord for us to bring forth for us of what the earth grows, its herbs, its cucumber its Fum, its lentils and its onions." He said, "Would you exchange that which is better for that which is lower Go you down to any town and you shall find what you want!" And they were covered with humiliation and misery, and they drew on themselves the wrath of Allah. That was because they used to disbelieve in the Ayat (proofs, evidence) of Allah and killed the Prophets wrongfully. That was because they disobeyed and used to transgress the bounds (in their disobedience to Allah, i.e. commit crimes and sins.)

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when ye said: O Moses! We are weary of one kind of food; so call upon thy Lord for us that He bring forth for us of that which the earth groweth - of its herbs and its cucumbers and its corn and its lentils and its onions. He said: Would ye exchange that which is higher for that which is lower? Go down to settled country, thus ye shall get that which ye demand. And humiliation and wretchedness were stamped upon them and they were visited with wrath from Allah. That was because they disbelieved in Allah's revelations and slew the prophets wrongfully. That was for their disobedience and transgression.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when you said, ‘O Moses, ‘We will not put up with one kind of food. So invoke your Lord for us, so that He may bring forth for us of that which the earth grows—its greens and cucumbers, its garlic, lentils, and onions.’ He said, ‘Do you seek to replace what is superior with that which is inferior? Go down to any town and you will indeed get what you ask for!’ So they were struck with abasement and poverty, and they earned Allah’s wrath. That, because they would deny the signs of Allah and kill the prophets unjustly. That, because they would disobey and commit transgressions.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Moses' you said, 'we will no longer be patient with one type of food. Call on your Lord to bring forth for us some of the produce of the earth, green herbs and cucumbers, corn, lentils and onions' 'What' he answered, 'Would you exchange that which is lesser for what is better? Go down into Egypt, there you shall find all that you have asked' Humiliation and abasement were pitched upon them, and they incurred the Anger of Allah; because they disbelieved His signs and slew His Prophets unjustly; because they disobeyed and were transgressors.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when you said, "O Moses, we can never endure one [kind of] food. So call upon your Lord to bring forth for us from the earth its green herbs and its cucumbers and its garlic and its lentils and its onions." [Moses] said, "Would you exchange what is better for what is less? Go into [any] settlement and indeed, you will have what you have asked." And they were covered with humiliation and poverty and returned with anger from Allah [upon them]. That was because they [repeatedly] disbelieved in the signs of Allah and killed the prophets without right. That was because they disobeyed and were [habitually] transgressing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When you demanded Moses to provide you with a variety of food, saying, "We no longer have patience with only one kind of food, ask your Lord to grow green herbs, cucumbers, corn, lentils, and onions for us," Moses replied, "Would you change what is good for what is worse? Go to any town and you will get what you want." Despised and afflicted with destitution, they brought the wrath of God back upon themselves, for they denied the evidence (of the existence of God) and murdered His Prophets without reason; they were disobedient transgressors.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when you said: O Musa! we cannot bear with one food, therefore pray Lord on our behalf to bring forth for us out of what the earth grows, of its herbs and its cucumbers and its garlic and its lentils and its onions. He said: Will you exchange that which is better for that which is worse? Enter a city, so you will have what you ask for. And abasement and humiliation were brought down upon them, and they became deserving of Allah's wrath; this was so because they disbelieved in the communications of Allah and killed the prophets unjustly; this was so because they disobeyed and exceeded the limits.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When you said, "Moses, we cannot bear to eat only one kind of food, so pray to your Lord to bring forth for us some of the earth's produce, its herbs and cucumbers, its garlic, lentils, and onions." Moses said, "Would you take a lesser thing in exchange for what is better? Go to some town and there you will find all that you demand." Abasement and destitution were stamped upon them, and they incurred the wrath of God, for having rejected His signs, and they killed His prophets unjustly, because they were rebels and transgressors.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember ye said: "O Moses! we cannot endure one kind of food (always); so beseech thy Lord for us to produce for us of what the earth groweth, -its pot-herbs, and cucumbers, Its garlic, lentils, and onions." He said: "Will ye exchange the better for the worse? Go ye down to any town, and ye shall find what ye want!" They were covered with humiliation and misery; they drew on themselves the wrath of Allah. This because they went on rejecting the Signs of Allah and slaying His Messengers without just cause. This because they rebelled and went on transgressing.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith qultum ya moosa lan nasbira AAala taAAamin wahidin faodAAu lana rabbaka yukhrij lana mimma tunbitu alardu min baqliha waqiththaiha wafoomiha waAAadasiha wabasaliha qala atastabdiloona allathee huwa adna biallathee huwa khayrun ihbitoo misran fainna lakum ma saaltum waduribat AAalayhimu alththillatu waalmaskanatu wabaoo bighadabin mina Allahi thalika biannahum kanoo yakfuroona biayati Allahi wayaqtuloona alnnabiyyeena bighayri alhaqqi thalika bima AAasaw wakanoo yaAAtadoona

62.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ وَٱلنَّصَـٰرَىٰ وَٱلصَّـٰبِـِٔينَ مَنْ ءَامَنَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Surely the believers and the Jews, Nazareans and the Sabians, whoever believes in God and the Last Day, and whosoever does right, shall have his reward with his Lord and will neither have fear nor regret.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed the believers (the Muslims) and those among the Jews, the Christians, and the Sabeans who sincerely accept faith in Allah and the Last Day* and do good deeds – their reward is with their Lord; and there shall be no fear upon them nor shall they grieve. (* i.e. convert to Islam)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Surely they that believe, and those of Jewry, and the Christians, and those Sabaeans, whoso believes in God and the Last Day, and works righteousness -- their wage awaits them with their Lord, and no fear shall be on them; neither shall they sorrow.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who believe, and those who are Judaised, and the Nazarenes, and the Sabians, - whosoever, believeth in Allah and the Last Day and worketh righteously, these! unto them shall be their hire with their Lord, no feal shall come on them nor shall they grieve.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! Those who believe and those who are Jews and Christians, and Sabians, whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day and do righteous good deeds shall have their reward with their Lord, on them shall be no fear, nor shall they grieve.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who believe, and those who are Jewish, and the Christians, and the Sabeans—any who believe in God and the Last Day, and act righteously—will have their reward with their Lord; they have nothing to fear, nor will they grieve.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Rest assured that whosoever from among the Muslims or the Jews or the Christians or the Sabaeans believes in Allah and the Last Day, and performs good deeds, he will have his reward with his Lord and he will have no cause for fear and grief.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who believe and those who are Jews and Christians, and Sabians (Sabi'in), whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day and does righteous good deeds shall have their reward with their Lord, on them shall be no fear, nor shall they grieve.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! Those who believe (in that which is revealed unto thee, Muhammad), and those who are Jews, and Christians, and Sabaeans - whoever believeth in Allah and the Last Day and doeth right - surely their reward is with their Lord, and there shall no fear come upon them neither shall they grieve.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed the faithful, the Jews, the Christians and the Sabaeans—those of them who have faith in Allah and the Last Day and act righteously—they shall have their reward from their Lord, and they will have no fear, nor will they grieve.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who believe, Jews, Nazarenes and Sabaeans whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day and does good deeds shall be rewarded by their Lord; they have nothing to fear nor are they saddened.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who believed and those who were Jews or Christians or Sabeans [before Prophet Muhammad] - those [among them] who believed in Allah and the Last Day and did righteousness - will have their reward with their Lord, and no fear will there be concerning them, nor will they grieve.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: However, those who have become believers (the Muslims), and the Jews, the Christians and the Sabaeans who believe in God and the Day of Judgment and strive righteously will receive their reward from the Lord and will have nothing to fear nor will they be grieved.
