translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely those who believe, and those who are Jews, and the Christians, and the Sabians, whoever believes in Allah and the Last day and does good, they shall have their reward from their Lord, and there is no fear for them, nor shall they grieve.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The believers, the Jews, the Christians, and the Sabaeans, all those who believe in God and the Last Day and do good deeds, will be rewarded by their Lord; they shall have no fear, nor shall they grieve.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who believe (in the Qur'an), and those who follow the Jewish (scriptures), and the Christians and the Sabians,- any who believe in Allah and the Last Day, and work righteousness, shall have their reward with their Lord; on them shall be no fear, nor shall they grieve.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena amanoo waallatheena hadoo waalnnasara waalssabieena man amana biAllahi waalyawmi alakhiri waAAamila salihan falahum ajruhum AAinda rabbihim wala khawfun AAalayhim wala hum yahzanoona

63.arabic: وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَـٰقَكُمْ وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَكُمُ ٱلطُّورَ خُذُوا۟ مَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰكُم بِقُوَّةٍ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ مَا فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember the day We made the covenant with you and exalted you on the Mount and said: "Hold fast to what We have given you, and remember what is therein that you may take heed."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when We made a covenant with you and raised the Mount above you; “Accept and hold fast to what We give you, and remember what is in it, so that you may attain piety.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when We took compact with you, and raised above you the Mount: 'Take forcefully what We have given you, and remember what is in it; haply you shall be godfearing.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time We took your bond, and raised over you the Tur saying: hold fast to that which We have vouchsafed unto you, and remember that which is therein, that haply ye may become God-fearing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (O Children of Israel, remember) when We took your covenant and We raised above you the Mount (saying): "Hold fast to that which We have given you, and remember that which is therein so that you may become Al-Muttaqun (the pious - see V. 2:2).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall when We received a pledge from you, and raised the Mount above you: “Take what We have given you earnestly, and remember what is in it, that you may attain righteousness.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Call to mind the time when We raised above you the Tur and made a covenant with you, saying, "Hold fast to the Book which We are giving you and bear in mind the commands and precepts contained therein. It is expected that this will lead you on to the paths of virtue and piety."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (O Children of Isra'il, remember) when We took your covenant and We raised above you the Mount (saying): "Hold fast to that which We have given you, and remember that which is therein so that you may acquire Taqwa.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And (remember, O Children of Israel) when We made a covenant with you and caused the mount to tower above you, (saying): Hold fast that which We have given you, and remember that which is therein, that ye may ward off (evil).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when We took a pledge from you, and raised the Mount above you, [declaring], ‘Hold on with power to what We have given you and remember that which is in it so that you may be Godwary.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when We made a covenant with you and raised the Mount above you, (saying) 'Take what We have given you forcefully and remember what is in it, so that you will be cautious'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when We took your covenant, [O Children of Israel, to abide by the Torah] and We raised over you the mount, [saying], "Take what We have given you with determination and remember what is in it that perhaps you may become righteous."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Children of Israel, recall when We made a covenant with you, raised Mount Tur (Sinai) above you and told you to receive earnestly what We had given to you and bear it in mind so that you would protect yourselves against evil.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when We took a promise from you and lifted the mountain over you: Take hold of the law (Tavrat) We have given you with firmness and bear in mind what is in it, so that you may guard (against evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: [Children of Israel], We made a covenant with you and raised the mountain high above you, saying, "Hold fast to what We have given you and bear its contents in mind, so that you may guard yourselves [against evil]."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember We took your covenant and We raised above you (The towering height) of Mount (Sinai): (Saying): "Hold firmly to what We have given you and bring (ever) to remembrance what is therein: Perchance ye may fear Allah."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith akhathna meethaqakum warafaAAna fawqakumu alttoora khuthoo ma ataynakum biquwwatin waothkuroo ma feehi laAAallakum tattaqoona

64.arabic: ثُمَّ تَوَلَّيْتُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ فَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ لَكُنتُم مِّنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But you went back (on your word), and but for the mercy and grace of God you were lost.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then after that, you turned away; and were it not for the munificence of Allah and His mercy, you would be among the losers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then you turned away thereafter, and but for the bounty and mercy of God towards you, you had been of the losers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then ye turned away there after; so had not the grace of Allah been unto you and His mercy, ye had surely become of the losers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then after that you turned away. Had it not been for the Grace and Mercy of Allah upon you, indeed you would have been among the losers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But after that you turned away. Were it not for God’s grace and mercy towards you, you would have been among the losers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But even after that you forsook the Covenant: nevertheless Allah did not withhold His grace and mercy from you; otherwise you would have been utterly ruined long before this.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then after that you turned away. Had it not been for the grace and mercy of Allah upon you, indeed you would have been among the losers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then, even after that, ye turned away, and if it had not been for the grace of Allah and His mercy ye had been among the losers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Again you turned away after that; and were it not for Allah’s grace on you and His mercy, you would have surely been among the losers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: yet after that you turned away, but for the Grace of Allah and His Mercy you would have surely been among the losers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then you turned away after that. And if not for the favor of Allah upon you and His mercy, you would have been among the losers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Again you turned away. Had God's Grace and His Mercy not existed in your favor, you would certainly have been lost.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then you turned back after that; so were it not for the grace of Allah and His mercy on you, you would certainly have been among the losers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Yet after that you turned away, and but for God's grace and mercy, you would have surely been among the losers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But ye turned back thereafter: Had it not been for the Grace and Mercy of Allah to you, ye had surely been among the lost.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thumma tawallaytum min baAAdi thalika falawla fadlu Allahi AAalaykum warahmatuhu lakuntum mina alkhasireena

65.arabic: وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْتُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱعْتَدَوْا۟ مِنكُمْ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ فَقُلْنَا لَهُمْ كُونُوا۟ قِرَدَةً خَـٰسِـِٔينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You know and have known already those among you who had broken the sanctity of the Sabbath, and to whom We had said: "Become (like) apes despised,"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And you certainly know of those amongst you who transgressed in the matter of Sabth (Sabbath – Saturday) – We therefore said to them, “Become apes, despised!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And well you know there were those among you that transgressed the Sabbath, and We said to them, 'Be you apes, miserably slinking!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly ye know of those of you who trespassed in the matter of the Sabbath, wherefore We said unto them: be ye apes despised.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And indeed you knew those amongst you who transgressed in the matter of the Sabbath (i.e. Saturday). We said to them: "Be you monkeys, despised and rejected."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And you surely knew those of you who violated the Sabbath. We said to them, “Be despicable apes!”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And you know well the story of those among you who broke Sabbath. We said to them, "Be apes despised and hated by all.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And indeed you knew those amongst you who transgressed in the matter of the Sabbath (i.e. Saturday). We said to them: "Be you monkeys, despised and rejected."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And ye know of those of you who broke the Sabbath, how We said unto them: Be ye apes, despised and hated!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And certainly you know those of you who violated the Sabbath, whereupon We said to them, ‘Be you spurned apes.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: You have surely known of those amongst you who transgressed the Sabbath. We said to them: 'Be apes, despised'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you had already known about those who transgressed among you concerning the sabbath, and We said to them, "Be apes, despised."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: You certainly knew about those among you who were transgressors on the Sabbath. We commanded them, "Become detested apes,"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And certainly you have known those among you who exceeded the limits of the Sabbath, so We said to them: Be (as) apes, despised and hated.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: You are aware of those who transgressed in the matter of the Sabbath, whereupon We said to them, "Be as apes, despised!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And well ye knew those amongst you who transgressed in the matter of the Sabbath: We said to them: "Be ye apes, despised and rejected."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqad AAalimtumu allatheena iAAtadaw minkum fee alssabti faqulna lahum koonoo qiradatan khasieena

66.arabic: فَجَعَلْنَـٰهَا نَكَـٰلًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهَا وَمَا خَلْفَهَا وَمَوْعِظَةً لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And whom We made an example for the people (of the day) and those after them, and warning for those who fear God.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So We made this incident (of that town) a warning to the surrounding towns (others of their time) and to succeeding generations, and a lesson for the pious.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We made it a punishment exemplary for all the former times and for the latter, and an admonition to such as are godfearing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We made it a deterrent unto those of their day and those after them, and an exhortation unto the God-fearing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So We made this punishment an example to their own and to succeeding generations and a lesson to those who are Al-Muttaqun (the pious - see V. 2:2).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Thus We made it a deterrent for their generation, and for subsequent generations, and a lesson for the righteous.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thus We made their end a warning to the people of their time and succeeding generations, and an admonition for God-fearing people.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So We made this punishment an example for those in front of it and those behind it, and a lesson for Al-Muttaqin (the pious).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We made it an example to their own and to succeeding generations, and an admonition to the Allah-fearing.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So We made it an exemplary punishment for the present and the succeeding [generations], and an advice to the Godwary.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We made it a punishment for the former times and for the latter, an exhortation to the cautious.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We made it a deterrent punishment for those who were present and those who succeeded [them] and a lesson for those who fear Allah.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: in order to set up an example for their contemporaries and coming generations and to make it a reminder for the pious.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So We made them an example to those who witnessed it and those who came after it, and an admonition to those who guard (against evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We made their fate an example to their own generation and to those who followed them and a lesson to those who fear God.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So We made it an example to their own time and to their posterity, and a lesson to those who fear Allah.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: FajaAAalnaha nakalan lima bayna yadayha wama khalfaha wamawAAithatan lilmuttaqeena

67.arabic: وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَن تَذْبَحُوا۟ بَقَرَةً ۖ قَالُوٓا۟ أَتَتَّخِذُنَا هُزُوًا ۖ قَالَ أَعُوذُ بِٱللَّهِ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, when Moses said to his people: "God demands that you sacrifice a cow," they said: "Are you making fun of us?" And he said: "God forbid that I be of the ignorant."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when Moosa said to his people, “Allah commands you to sacrifice a cow”; they said, “Are you making fun of us?” He answered, “Allah forbid that I should be of the ignorant!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when Moses said to his people, 'God commands you to sacrifice a cow.' They said, 'Dost thou take us in mockery?' He said, 'I take refuge with God, lest I should be one of the ignorant.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time Musa said unto his people: verily Allah commandeth you that ye slaughter a cow. They said: makest thou a jest of us? He said take refuge with Allah that I should be of the ignorant.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when Musa (Moses) said to his people: "Verily, Allah commands you that you slaughter a cow." They said, "Do you make fun of us?" He said, "I take Allah's Refuge from being among Al-Jahilun (the ignorants or the foolish)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall when Moses said to his people, “God commands you to sacrifice a heifer.” They said, “Do you make a mockery of us?” He said, “God forbid that I should be so ignorant.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then call to mind the other event: when Moses said to his people, "Allah commands you to sacrifice a cow," they replied, "Do you mean to have a jest with us?" He answered, "I crave Allah's protection from behaving like ignorant people."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when Musa said to his people: "Verily, Allah commands you that you slaughter a cow." They said, "Do you make fun of us" He said, "I take Allah's refuge from being among Al-Jahilin (the ignorant or the foolish)."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when Moses said unto his people: Lo! Allah commandeth you that ye sacrifice a cow, they said: Dost thou make game of us? He answered: Allah forbid that I should be among the foolish!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when Moses said to his people, ‘Allah commands you to slaughter a cow,’ they said, ‘Are you mocking us?’ He said, ‘I seek Allah’s protection lest I should be one of the ignorant!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When Moses said to his nation: 'Allah commands you to slaughter a cow' they replied: 'Are you taking us in mockery' 'I seek protection with Allah lest I should be one of the ignorant' he said.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when Moses said to his people, "Indeed, Allah commands you to slaughter a cow." They said, "Do you take us in ridicule?" He said, "I seek refuge in Allah from being among the ignorant."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When Moses said to his people, "God commands you to sacrifice a cow," they asked, "Are you mocking us?" "God forbid, how would I be so ignorant," said Moses.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when Musa said to his people: Surely Allah commands you that you should sacrifice a cow; they said: Do you ridicule us? He said: I seek the protection of Allah from being one of the ignorant.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When Moses said to his people, "God commands you to sacrifice a heifer," they said, "Are you making a mockery of us?" He answered, "God forbid that I should be so ignorant!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And remember Moses said to his people: "Allah commands that ye sacrifice a heifer." They said: "Makest thou a laughing-stock of us?" He said: "Allah save me from being an ignorant (fool)!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith qala moosa liqawmihi inna Allaha yamurukum an tathbahoo baqaratan qaloo atattakhithuna huzuwan qala aAAoothu biAllahi an akoona mina aljahileena

68.arabic: قَالُوا۟ ٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا هِىَ ۚ قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ يَقُولُ إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا فَارِضٌ وَلَا بِكْرٌ عَوَانٌۢ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ فَٱفْعَلُوا۟ مَا تُؤْمَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "Call on your Lord for us," they said, "that He might inform us what kind she should be." "Neither old nor young, says God, but of age in between," answered Moses. "So do as you are bid."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “Pray to your Lord that He may describe the cow”; said Moosa, “He says that it is a cow neither old nor very young but between the two conditions; so do what you are commanded.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Pray to thy Lord for us, that He may make clear to us what she may be.' He said, 'He says she is a cow neither old, nor virgin, middling between the two; so do that you are bidden.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: supplicate for us unto thy Lord that He make manifest unto us whatever she should be. He said: verily He saith, she should be a cow neither old nor young, but middle-aged betwixt that; do then as ye are commanded.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said, "Call upon your Lord for us that He may make plain to us what it is!" He said, "He says, 'Verily, it is a cow neither too old nor too young, but (it is) between the two conditions', so do what you are commanded."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “Call upon your Lord to show us which one.” He said, “He says she is a heifer, neither too old, nor too young, but in between. So do what you are commanded.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then they said, "Please make a request to your Lord to give us some details of the cow." Moses answered, "Allah says that the cow should neither be old nor immature but of middle age. Do, therefore, as you are bidden."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said, "Call upon your Lord for us that He may make plain to us what it is!" He said, "He says, `Verily, it is a cow neither too old nor too young, but (it is) between the two conditions', so do what you are commanded."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: Pray for us unto thy Lord that He make clear to us what (cow) she is. (Moses) answered: Lo! He saith, Verily she is a cow neither with calf nor immature; (she is) between the two conditions; so do that which ye are commanded.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘Invoke your Lord for us that He may clarify for us what she may be.’ He said, ‘He says, she is a cow, neither old nor young, of a middle age. Now do what you are commanded.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Call on your Lord' they said, 'to make known to us what she shall be' He said: "He says she is a cow neither old, nor virgin, middling between both." Therefore, do as you have been ordered'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "Call upon your Lord to make clear to us what it is." [Moses] said, "[Allah] says, 'It is a cow which is neither old nor virgin, but median between that,' so do what you are commanded."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They demanded, "Ask your Lord to describe the kind of cow He wants us to slaughter." Moses explained, "It must be neither too old nor too young, thus do whatever you are commanded to do." Moses then told them to do as they were ordered.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: Call on your Lord for our sake to make it plain to us what she is. Musa said: He says, Surely she is a cow neither advanced in age nor too young, of middle age between that (and this); do therefore what you are commanded.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They said, "Ask your Lord to make it clear to us what sort of heifer she should be." He answered, "God says she should be neither too old nor too young, but in between, so do as you are commanded."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "Beseech on our behalf Thy Lord to make plain to us what (heifer) it is!" He said; "He says: The heifer should be neither too old nor too young, but of middling age. Now do what ye are commanded!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo odAAu lana rabbaka yubayyin lana ma hiya qala innahu yaqoolu innaha baqaratun la faridun wala bikrun AAawanun bayna thalika faifAAaloo ma tumaroona

69.arabic: قَالُوا۟ ٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا لَوْنُهَا ۚ قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ يَقُولُ إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ صَفْرَآءُ فَاقِعٌ لَّوْنُهَا تَسُرُّ ٱلنَّـٰظِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "Call on your Lord," they said, "to tell us the colour of the cow." "God says," answered Moses, "a fawn coloured cow, rich yellow, well pleasing to the eye."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “Pray to your Lord that He may reveal its colour to us”; answered Moosa, “Indeed He says it is a yellow cow, of bright colour, pleasing to the beholders.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Pray to thy Lord for us, that He make clear to us what her colour may be.' He said, 'He says she shall be a golden cow, bright her colour, gladdening the beholders.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: supplicate for us unto thy Lord that He make manifest unto us whatever her colour should be. He said: verily He saith, she should be a yellow cow the colour whereof is deepest, delighting the beholders.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said, "Call upon your Lord for us to make plain to us its colour." He said, "He says, 'It is a yellow cow, bright in its colour, pleasing to the beholders.' "

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “Call upon your Lord to show us what her color is.” He said, “He says she is a yellow heifer, bright in color, pleasing to the beholders.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But they further asked, "Please request your Lord to make it clear to us of what colour she should be." Moses answered, "He says that she should be of yellow colour, so deep and bright as to delight the beholders."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said, "Call upon your Lord for us to make plain to us its colour." He said, "He says, `It is a yellow cow, bright in its colour, pleasing the beholders.' "

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: Pray for us unto thy Lord that He make clear to us of what colour she is. (Moses) answered: Lo! He saith: Verily she is a yellow cow. Bright is her colour, gladdening beholders.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘Invoke your Lord for us that He may clarify for us what her colour may be.’ He said, ‘He says, she is a cow that is yellow, of a bright hue, pleasing to the onlookers.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Call on your Lord' they said, 'to make known to us what her color shall be' 'Your Lord says: "The cow is yellow, a rich yellow pleasing to the onlookers.'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "Call upon your Lord to show us what is her color." He said, "He says, 'It is a yellow cow, bright in color - pleasing to the observers.' "

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They further demanded Moses to ask the Lord what color the cow has to be. Moses answered, "The Lord says that the cow must be yellow, a beautiful yellow".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: Call on your Lord for our sake to make it plain to us what her color is. Musa said: He says, Surely she is a yellow cow; her color is intensely yellow, giving delight to the beholders.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They said, "Call on your Lord for us, to show us what colour she should be." He answered, "God says she should be a bright yellow heifer, pleasing to the eye."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "Beseech on our behalf Thy Lord to make plain to us Her colour." He said: "He says: A fawn-coloured heifer, pure and rich in tone, the admiration of beholders!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo odAAu lana rabbaka yubayyin lana ma lawnuha qala innahu yaqoolu innaha baqaratun safrao faqiAAun lawnuha tasurru alnnathireena

70.arabic: قَالُوا۟ ٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُبَيِّن لَّنَا مَا هِىَ إِنَّ ٱلْبَقَرَ تَشَـٰبَهَ عَلَيْنَا وَإِنَّآ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَمُهْتَدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "Call on your Lord," they said, "to name its variety, as cows be all alike to us. If God wills we shall be guided aright."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “Pray to your Lord that He may clearly describe the cow to us, we are really in a doubt as to which cow it is; and if Allah wills, we will attain guidance.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Pray to thy Lord for us, that He make clear to us what she may be; cows are much alike to us; and, if God will, we shall then be guided.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said supplicate for us unto thy Lord that He make manifest unto us whatever she should be, for verily the cow hath become dubious unto us and verily we, if God will, shall beguided.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said, "Call upon your Lord for us to make plain to us what it is. Verily to us all cows are alike, And surely, if Allah wills, we will be guided."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “Call upon your Lord to show us which one; the heifers look alike to us; and God willing, we will be guided.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Again they said, "Pray your Lord to specify for us the kind of cow that is required; for cows (of this type) look alike to us. We shall then find her, if God so wills."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said, "Call upon your Lord for us to make plain to us what it is. Verily, to us all cows are alike. And surely, if Allah wills, we will be guided."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: Pray for us unto thy Lord that He make clear to us what (cow) she is. Lo! cows are much alike to us; and Lo! if Allah wills, we may be led aright.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘Invoke your Lord for us that He may clarify for us what she may be. Indeed all cows are much alike to us, and if Allah wishes we will surely be guided.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Call on your Lord' they said, 'to make known to us which cow she shall be; to us cows look alike. If Allah wills, we shall be guided'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "Call upon your Lord to make clear to us what it is. Indeed, [all] cows look alike to us. And indeed we, if Allah wills, will be guided."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They said, "We are confused about the cow, for to us all cows look alike. Ask your Lord to tell us exactly what the cow looks like, so that God willing, we shall have the right description."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: Call on your Lord for our sake to make it plain to us what she is, for surely to us the cows are all alike, and if Allah please we shall surely be guided aright.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They said, "Ask your Lord to make it clear to us the exact type of heifer she should be, for to us all heifers look alike. If God wills, we shall be rightly guided."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "Beseech on our behalf Thy Lord to make plain to us what she is: To us are all heifers alike: We wish indeed for guidance, if Allah wills."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo odAAu lana rabbaka yubayyin lana ma hiya inna albaqara tashabaha AAalayna wainna in shaa Allahu lamuhtadoona

71.arabic: قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ يَقُولُ إِنَّهَا بَقَرَةٌ لَّا ذَلُولٌ تُثِيرُ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَلَا تَسْقِى ٱلْحَرْثَ مُسَلَّمَةٌ لَّا شِيَةَ فِيهَا ۚ قَالُوا۟ ٱلْـَٔـٰنَ جِئْتَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ فَذَبَحُوهَا وَمَا كَادُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And Moses said: "He says it's a cow unyoked, nor worn out by ploughing or watering the fields, one in good shape with no mark or blemish." "Now have you brought us the truth," they said; and then, after wavering, they sacrificed the cow.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Said Moosa, “He says, ‘She is a cow not made to work, neither ploughing the soil nor watering the fields; flawless and spotless’; they said, “You have now conveyed the proper fact”; so they sacrificed it, but seemed not to be sacrificing it (with sincerity).

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He said, 'He says she shall be a cow not broken to plough the earth or to water the tillage, one kept secure, with no blemish on her.' They said, 'Now thou hast brought the truth'; and therefore they sacrificed her, a thing they had scarcely done.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: verily He saith, verify she should be a cow unyoked, not broken to till the ground or to water the field, whole, and without blemish in her. They said: now hast thou brought the truth. Then they slaughtered her, and they were wellnigh not doing it.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He [Musa (Moses)] said, "He says, 'It is a cow neither trained to till the soil nor water the fields, sound, having no other colour except bright yellow.' " They said, "Now you have brought the truth." So they slaughtered it though they were near to not doing it.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “He says she is a heifer, neither yoked to plow the earth, nor to irrigate the field; sound without blemish.” They said, “Now you have brought the truth.” So they slew her; though they almost did not.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Moses answered, "Allah says that she should be a cow which has not been yoked nor has ploughed the land nor watered the fields; which is sound and whole, without belemish. " Then they cried out, "Now you have given an accurate description. " Then they sacrificed her but they did not appear to be doing this willingly.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He (Musa) said, "He says, `It is a cow neither trained to till the soil nor water the fields, sound, having no blemish in it.' " They said, "Now you have brought the truth." So they slaughtered it though they were near to not doing it.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (Moses) answered: Lo! He saith: Verily she is a cow unyoked; she plougheth not the soil nor watereth the tilth; whole and without mark. They said: Now thou bringest the truth. So they sacrificed her, though almost they did not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘He says, She is a cow not broken to till the earth or water the tillage, sound and without blemish.’ They said, ‘Now have you come up with the truth!’ And they slaughtered it, though they were about not to do it.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He replied: 'He says: "She is a cow, neither worn out plowing the earth nor watering the field, one kept secure free from any blemish.' 'Now you have brought us the truth' they answered. And they slaughtered her, after they had been reluctant to do so.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He said, "He says, 'It is a cow neither trained to plow the earth nor to irrigate the field, one free from fault with no spot upon her.' " They said, "Now you have come with the truth." So they slaughtered her, but they could hardly do it.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Moses) said, "The Lord says that it must not have even tilled the soil nor irrigated the fields and it must be free of blemishes and flaws." They said, "Now you have given us the right description." After almost failing to find it, they slaughtered the cow.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Musa said: He says, Surely she is a cow not made submissive that she should plough the land, nor does she irrigate the tilth; sound, without a blemish in her. They said: Now you have brought the truth; so they sacrificed her, though they had not the mind to do (it).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Moses answered, "Your Lord says, she should be a heifer which has not been yoked to plough the land or water the fields, a heifer free from any blemish." "Now you have told us the truth," they answered, and they sacrificed it, though they would rather not have done it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "He says: A heifer not trained to till the soil or water the fields; sound and without blemish." They said: "Now hast thou brought the truth." Then they offered her in sacrifice, but not with good-will.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala innahu yaqoolu innaha baqaratun la thaloolun tutheeru alarda wala tasqee alhartha musallamatun la shiyata feeha qaloo alana jita bialhaqqi fathabahooha wama kadoo yafAAaloona

72.arabic: وَإِذْ قَتَلْتُمْ نَفْسًا فَٱدَّٰرَْٰٔتُمْ فِيهَا ۖ وَٱللَّهُ مُخْرِجٌ مَّا كُنتُمْ تَكْتُمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember when you killed a man and blamed each other for the deed, God brought to light what you concealed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when you slew a man and were therefore accusing each other concerning it; and Allah wanted to expose what you were hiding.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when you killed a living soul, and disputed thereon -- and God disclosed what you were hiding --

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And recall what time ye slew a person, then quarrelled among yourselves respecting it, and Allah was to bring out that which ye were hiding.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) when you killed a man and fell into dispute among yourselves as to the crime. But Allah brought forth that which you were hiding.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And recall when you killed a person, and disputed in the matter; but God was to expose what you were hiding.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: You should also recall to mind another incident: You slew a man and began to dispute about the murder and accuse one another of it, but Allah had decreed that what you were trying to hide should be disclosed.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) when you killed a man and disagreed among yourselves as to the crime. But Allah brought forth that which you were Taktumun.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And (remember) when ye slew a man and disagreed concerning it and Allah brought forth that which ye were hiding.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And when you killed a soul, and accused one another about it—and Allah was to expose what you were concealing—

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And when you slew a soul and then fell out with one another concerning it, Allah made known what you concealed.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [recall] when you slew a man and disputed over it, but Allah was to bring out that which you were concealing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When you murdered someone, each one of you tried to accuse others of being guilty. However, God made public what you were hiding.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when you killed a man, then you disagreed with respect to that, and Allah was to bring forth that which you were going to hide.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then, when you [Israelites] killed someone and started to blame one another, God brought to light what you concealed,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Remember ye slew a man and fell into a dispute among yourselves as to the crime: But Allah was to bring forth what ye did hide.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waith qataltum nafsan faiddaratum feeha waAllahu mukhrijun ma kuntum taktumoona

73.arabic: فَقُلْنَا ٱضْرِبُوهُ بِبَعْضِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُحْىِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَيُرِيكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We had pronounced already: "Slay (the murderer) for (taking a life)." Thus God preserves life from death and shows you His signs that you may understand.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We therefore said, “Strike the dead man with a part of the sacrificed cow”; this is how Allah will bring the dead to life, and shows you His signs so that you may understand!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: so We said, 'Smite him with part of it'; even so God brings to life the dead, and He shows you His signs, that haply you may have understanding.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore We said: smite him with part of her. Thus will Allah quicken the dead and He sheweth you His signs that haply ye may under stand.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So We said: "Strike him (the dead man) with a piece of it (the cow)." Thus Allah brings the dead to life and shows you His Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) so that you may understand.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We said, “Strike him with part of it.” Thus God brings the dead to life; and He shows you His signs, that you may understand.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So We commanded, "Strike the corpse of the murdered man with a part of the sacrificed cow. See how Allah brings the dead to life and shows you His Signs, so that you may understand".

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So We said: "Strike him (the dead man) with a piece of it (the cow)." Thus Allah brings the dead to life and shows you His Ayat (proofs, evidences, etc.) so that you may understand.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We said: Smite him with some of it. Thus Allah bringeth the dead to life and showeth you His portents so that ye may understand.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We said, ‘Strike him with a piece of it:’ thus does Allah revive the dead, and He shows you His signs so that you may exercise your reason.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We said: 'Strike him with a piece of it' Like this, Allah restores the dead to life and shows you His signs in order that you will understand.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So, We said, "Strike the slain man with part of it." Thus does Allah bring the dead to life, and He shows you His signs that you might reason.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We said, "Strike the person slain with some part of the cow." That is how God brings the dead to life and shows you His miracles so that you might have understanding.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So We said: Strike the (dead body) with part of the (Sacrificed cow), thus Allah brings the dead to life, and He shows you His signs so that you may understand.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We said, "Strike [the corpse] with a part of it." Thus God restores the dead to life and shows you His signs, so that you may understand.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So We said: "Strike the (body) with a piece of the (heifer)." Thus Allah bringeth the dead to life and showeth you His Signs: Perchance ye may understand.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faqulna idriboohu bibaAAdiha kathalika yuhyee Allahu almawta wayureekum ayatihi laAAallakum taAAqiloona

74.arabic: ثُمَّ قَسَتْ قُلُوبُكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ فَهِىَ كَٱلْحِجَارَةِ أَوْ أَشَدُّ قَسْوَةً ۚ وَإِنَّ مِنَ ٱلْحِجَارَةِ لَمَا يَتَفَجَّرُ مِنْهُ ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۚ وَإِنَّ مِنْهَا لَمَا يَشَّقَّقُ فَيَخْرُجُ مِنْهُ ٱلْمَآءُ ۚ وَإِنَّ مِنْهَا لَمَا يَهْبِطُ مِنْ خَشْيَةِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet, in spite of this, your hearts only hardened like rocks or even harder, but among rocks are those from which rivers flow; and there are also those which split open and water gushes forth; as well as those that roll down for fear of God. And God is not negligent of all that you do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then after it, your hearts hardened – so they are like rocks, or even harder; for there are some rocks that rivers gush forth from them; and some that water flows from them when they split asunder; and there are rocks that fall down for the fear of Allah; and Allah is not unaware of your deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then your hearts became hardened thereafter and are like stones, or even yet harder; for there are stones from which rivers come gushing, and others split, so that water issues from them, and others crash down in the fear of God. And God is not heedless of the things you do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then your hearts hardened thereafter, so they are as stones or stronger in hardness: and verily of stones there are some from which gush forth rivers, and verily there are of them some that cleave asunder and water issueth therefrom, and verily there are of them some that fall down in awe of Allah: and Allah is not neglectful of that which ye work.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then, after that, your hearts were hardened and became as stones or even worse in hardness. And indeed, there are stones out of which rivers gush forth, and indeed, there are of them (stones) which split asunder so that water flows from them, and indeed, there are of them (stones) which fall down for fear of Allah. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then after that your hearts hardened. They were as rocks, or even harder. For there are some rocks from which rivers gush out, and others that splinter and water comes out from them, and others that sink in awe of God. God is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But even after seeing these Signs your hearts hardened and became as hard as rocks; nay, even harder than rocks. For there are some rocks out of which springs gush forth, and others which split open, and water issues out of them; then there are some which tumble down for fear of Allah. And Allah is not unaware of what you are doing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then after that your hearts were hardened and became as stones or even worse in hardness. And indeed, there are stones out of which rivers gush forth, and indeed, there are of them (stones) which split asunder so that water flows from them, and indeed, there are of them (stones) which fall down for fear of Allah. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then, even after that, your hearts were hardened and became as rocks, or worse than rocks, for hardness. For indeed there are rocks from out which rivers gush, and indeed there are rocks which split asunder so that water floweth from them. And indeed there are rocks which fall down for the fear of Allah. Allah is not unaware of what ye do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then your hearts hardened after that; so they are like stones, or even harder. For there are some stones from which streams gush forth, and there are some of them that split, and water issues from them, and there are some that fall for the fear of Allah. And Allah is not oblivious of what you do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Yet after that your hearts became as hard as rock or even harder. Indeed among the stones are those from which rivers burst. And others split so that water issues from them; and others crash down through fear of Allah. Allah is not inattentive of what you do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then your hearts became hardened after that, being like stones or even harder. For indeed, there are stones from which rivers burst forth, and there are some of them that split open and water comes out, and there are some of them that fall down for fear of Allah. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Thereafter, your hearts turned as hard as rocks or even harder for some rocks give way to the streams to flow. Water comes out of some rocks when they are torn apart and others tumble down in awe before God. God does not ignore what you do.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then your hearts hardened after that, so that they were like rocks, rather worse in hardness; and surely there are some rocks from which streams burst forth, and surely there are some of them which split asunder so water issues out of them, and surely there are some of them which fall down for fear of Allah, and Allah is not at all heedless of what you do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then your hearts became as hard as rock or even harder: for, there are rocks from which streams gush forth; and, there are some that cleave asunder and water flows out from them, while others fall down for fear of God. God is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Thenceforth were your hearts hardened: They became like a rock and even worse in hardness. For among rocks there are some from which rivers gush forth; others there are which when split asunder send forth water; and others which sink for fear of Allah. And Allah is not unmindful of what ye do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thumma qasat quloobukum min baAAdi thalika fahiya kaalhijarati aw ashaddu qaswatan wainna mina alhijarati lama yatafajjaru minhu alanharu wainna minha lama yashshaqqaqu fayakhruju minhu almao wainna minha lama yahbitu min khashyati Allahi wama Allahu bighafilin AAamma taAAmaloona

75.arabic: أَفَتَطْمَعُونَ أَن يُؤْمِنُوا۟ لَكُمْ وَقَدْ كَانَ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ كَلَـٰمَ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ يُحَرِّفُونَهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا عَقَلُوهُ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: How do you expect them to put their faith in you, when you know that some among them heard the word of God and, having understood, perverted it knowingly?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So O Muslims, do you wish for the Jews to accept faith in you whereas a group of them used to listen to the Words of Allah, and then after having understood it, purposely changed it?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Are you then so eager that they should believe you, seeing there is a party of them that heard God's word, and then tampered with it, and that after they had comprehended it, wittingly?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Covet ye then that they would believe for you whereas surely a Party of them hath been hearing the Word of Allah and then reverting it after they have understood it, While they know.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Do you (faithful believers) covet that they will believe in your religion inspite of the fact that a party of them (Jewish rabbis) used to hear the Word of Allah [the Taurat (Torah)], then they used to change it knowingly after they understood it?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Do you hope that they will believe in you, when some of them used to hear the Word of God, and then deliberately distort it, even after understanding it?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: O Muslims, do you then expect that these people will accept your invitation and become believers? whereas there have always been among them some who have been hearing the Word of God, understanding it well and then perverting and tampering with it knowingly.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Do you (faithful believers) covet that they will believe in your religion inspite of the fact that a party of them (Jewish rabbis) used to hear the Word of Allah (the Tawrah), then they used to change it knowingly after they understood it

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Have ye any hope that they will be true to you when a party of them used to listen to the word of Allah, then used to change it, after they had understood it, knowingly?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Are you then eager that they should believe you, though a part of them would hear the word of Allah and then they would distort it after they had understood it, and they knew [what they were doing]?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do you then hope that they will believe in you, when some of them have already heard the Word of Allah and knowingly tampered with it, after they understood it!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Do you covet [the hope, O believers], that they would believe for you while a party of them used to hear the words of Allah and then distort the Torah after they had understood it while they were knowing?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do you, the believers in truth, desire the unbelievers to believe you? There was a group among them who would hear the word of God and understand it. Then they would purposely misinterpret it.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Do you then hope that they would believe in you, and a party from among them indeed used to hear the Word of Allah, then altered it after they had understood it, and they know (this).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do you then hope that they will believe in you when some of them have already heard the word of God and then, after having understood it, they knowingly perverted it?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Can ye (o ye men of Faith) entertain the hope that they will believe in you?- Seeing that a party of them heard the Word of Allah, and perverted it knowingly after they understood it.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: AfatatmaAAoona an yuminoo lakum waqad kana fareequn minhum yasmaAAoona kalama Allahi thumma yuharrifoonahu min baAAdi ma AAaqaloohu wahum yaAAlamoona

76.arabic: وَإِذَا لَقُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَا بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ قَالُوٓا۟ أَتُحَدِّثُونَهُم بِمَا فَتَحَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لِيُحَآجُّوكُم بِهِۦ عِندَ رَبِّكُمْ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: For when they meet the faithful, they say: "We believe;" but when among themselves, they say: "Why do you tell them what the Lord has revealed to you? They will only dispute it in the presence of your Lord. Have you no sense indeed?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when they meet the believers, they say, “We believe”; but when they are in isolation with one another they say, “You clarify to the believers from what Allah has disclosed to you, so that they may evidence it against you before your Lord? So have you no sense?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And when they meet those who believe, they say 'We believe'; and when they go privily one to another, they say, 'Do you speak to them of what God has revealed to you, that they may thereby dispute with you before your Lord? Have you no understanding?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when they meet those who believe they say: we believe; and when some of them are alone with some others they say speak ye unto them of that which God hath opened unto you so that they may argue therewith against you before your Lord? understand then ye not?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when they (Jews) meet those who believe (Muslims), they say, "We believe", but when they meet one another in private, they say, "Shall you (Jews) tell them (Muslims) what Allah has revealed to you [Jews, about the description and the qualities of Prophet Muhammad Peace be upon him, that which are written in the Taurat (Torah)], that they (Muslims) may argue with you (Jews) about it before your Lord?" Have you (Jews) then no understanding?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And when they come across those who believe, they say, “We believe,” but when they come together privately, they say, “Will you inform them of what God has disclosed to you, so that they might dispute with you concerning it before your Lord?” Do you not understand?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When they meet those who believe in Muhammad, they say, "We also believe in him." But when they meet one another in private, they say, "Have you got no sense that you disclose to them those things which Allah has revealed to you so that they might bring them as a proof against you before your Lord?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when they (Jews) meet those who believe (Muslims), they say, "We believe," but when they meet one another in private, they say, "Shall you (Jews) tell them (Muslims) what Allah has revealed to you that they (Muslims) may argue with you (Jews) about it before your Lord" Have you (Jews) then no understanding

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when they fall in with those who believe, they say: We believe. But when they go apart one with another they say: Prate ye to them of that which Allah hath disclosed to you that they may contend with you before your Lord concerning it? Have ye then no sense?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When they meet the faithful, they say, ‘We believe,’ and when they are alone with one another, they say, ‘Do you recount to them what Allah has revealed to you, so that they may argue with you therewith before your Lord? Do you not exercise your reason?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When they meet those who believe, they say: 'We are believers' But when alone, they say to their other (chiefs). 'Do you tell to them what Allah has revealed to you so that they will dispute with you concerning it with your Lord? Have you no sense'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when they meet those who believe, they say, "We have believed"; but when they are alone with one another, they say, "Do you talk to them about what Allah has revealed to you so they can argue with you about it before your Lord?" Then will you not reason?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: On meeting the believers, they would declare belief but to each other they would say, "How would you (against your own interests) tell them (believers) about what God has revealed to you (in the Bible of the truthfulness of the Prophet Muhammad)? They will present it as evidence to prove you wrong before your Lord. Do you not realize it?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when they meet those who believe they say: We believe, and when they are alone one with another they say: Do you talk to them of what Allah has disclosed to you that they may contend with you by this before your Lord? Do you not then understand?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When they meet the faithful, they say, "We are believers." But when alone, they say to each other, "Must you tell them what God has revealed to us? They will only use it to argue against you before your Lord! Have you no sense?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Behold! when they meet the men of Faith, they say: "We believe": But when they meet each other in private, they say: "Shall you tell them what Allah hath revealed to you, that they may engage you in argument about it before your Lord?"- Do ye not understand (their aim)?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waitha laqoo allatheena amanoo qaloo amanna waitha khala baAAduhum ila baAAdin qaloo atuhaddithoonahum bima fataha Allahu AAalaykum liyuhajjookum bihi AAinda rabbikum afala taAAqiloona

77.arabic: أَوَلَا يَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا يُسِرُّونَ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do they not know that God is aware of what they hide and what they disclose?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Do they not know that Allah knows all whatever they hide and whatever they disclose?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Know they not that God knows what they keep secret and what they publish?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Know they not that Allah knoweth that which they hide and that which they make known?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Know they (Jews) not that Allah knows what they conceal and what they reveal?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Do they not know that God knows what they conceal and what they reveal?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do they really not know that Allah is fully aware of what they hide and what they disclose?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Know they (Jews) not that Allah knows what they conceal and what they reveal

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Are they then unaware that Allah knoweth that which they keep hidden and that which they proclaim?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they not know that Allah knows whatever they hide and whatever they disclose?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do they not know that Allah has knowledge of all they hide and all that they reveal!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But do they not know that Allah knows what they conceal and what they declare?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do they not know that God knows whatever they conceal or reveal?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Do they not know that Allah knows what they keep secret and what they make known?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do they not know that God knows all that they conceal and all that they disclose?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Know they not that Allah knoweth what they conceal and what they reveal?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Awala yaAAlamoona anna Allaha yaAAlamu ma yusirroona wama yuAAlinoona

78.arabic: وَمِنْهُمْ أُمِّيُّونَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ إِلَّآ أَمَانِىَّ وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَظُنُّونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Among them are heathens who know nothing of the Book but only what they wish to believe, and are only lost in fantasies.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And among them are the unlearned that do not know anything of the Book except to recite something therefrom or parts of their own fabrications; they are in absolute illusion.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And some there are of them that are common folk not knowing the Book, but only fancies and mere conjectures.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And of them are unlettered ones who know not the Book but vain desires; and they do but conjecture.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And there are among them (Jews) unlettered people, who know not the Book, but they trust upon false desires and they but guess.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And among them are uneducated who know the Scripture only through hearsay, and they only speculate.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then there are among them some un-lettered people who have no knowledge of the Book but depend upon empty hopes and are guided by mere conjecture and guess-work.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And there are among them (Jews) `Ummyyun (unlettered) people, who know not the Book, but they trust upon Amani (false desires) and they but guess.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Among them are unlettered folk who know the Scripture not except from hearsay. They but guess.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And among them are the illiterate who know nothing of the Book except hearsay, and they only make conjectures.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And some of them are common (people) and do not know the Book, but only wishful thoughts, and they are only doubters.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And among them are unlettered ones who do not know the Scripture except in wishful thinking, but they are only assuming.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Some of them are illiterate and have no knowledge of the Book except for what they know from legends and fantasy. They are only relying on conjecture.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And there are among them illiterates who know not the Book but only lies, and they do but conjecture.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: There are among them unlettered people who have no real knowledge of the Scriptures, knowing only their own wishful thinking and following only conjecture.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And there are among them illiterates, who know not the Book, but (see therein their own) desires, and they do nothing but conjecture.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waminhum ommiyyoona la yaAAlamoona alkitaba illa amaniyya wain hum illa yathunnoona

79.arabic: فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَكْتُبُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ ثُمَّ يَقُولُونَ هَـٰذَا مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ لِيَشْتَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۖ فَوَيْلٌ لَّهُم مِّمَّا كَتَبَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَوَيْلٌ لَّهُم مِّمَّا يَكْسِبُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But woe to them who fake the Scriptures and say: "This is from God," so that they might earn some profit thereby; and woe to them for what they fake, and woe to them for what they earn from it!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore woe is to those who write the Book with their hands; and they then claim, “This is from Allah” in order to gain an abject (worldly) price for it; therefore woe to them for what their hands have written, and woe to them for what they earn with it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So woe to those who write the Book with their hands, then say, 'This is from God,' that they may sell it for a little price; so woe to them for what their hands have written, and woe to them for their earnings.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Woe then unto those who write out the Book with their hands and say thereafter: this is from God, that they may barter it for a small price. Woe then unto them for that which thei hands had written; and woe unto them for that which they earn therwith.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then woe to those who write the Book with their own hands and then say, "This is from Allah," to purchase with it a little price! Woe to them for what their hands have written and woe to them for that they earn thereby.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So woe to those who write the Scripture with their own hands, and then say, “This is from God,” that they may exchange it for a little price. Woe to them for what their hands have written, and woe to them for what they earn.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So woe to their learned people, who write the law with their own hands and then say to the people, "This is from Allah," so that they might gain some paltry worldly end. (They do not see that) this writing of their hands will bring woe to them and what they gain thereby will lead to their ruin.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then woe to those who write the book with their own hands and then say, "This is from Allah," to purchase with it a little price! Woe to them for what their hands have written and woe to them for that they earn thereby.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Therefore woe be unto those who write the Scripture with their hands and then say, "This is from Allah," that they may purchase a small gain therewith. Woe unto them for that their hands have written, and woe unto them for that they earn thereby.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So woe to them who write the Book with their hands and then say, ‘This is from Allah,’ that they may sell it for a paltry gain. So woe to them for what their hands have written, and woe to them for what they earn!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Woe to those who write the Book with their own hands and then say: 'This is from Allah' in order to gain a small price for it. So woe to them for what their hands have written, and woe to them for their earnings.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So woe to those who write the "scripture" with their own hands, then say, "This is from Allah," in order to exchange it for a small price. Woe to them for what their hands have written and woe to them for what they earn.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Woe to those who write the Book themselves and say, "This is from God," so that they may sell it for a small price! Woe unto them for what they have done and for what they have gained!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Woe, then, to those who write the book with their hands and then say: This is from Allah, so that they may take for it a small price; therefore woe to them for what their hands have written and woe to them for what they earn.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Woe to those who write the scripture with their own hands, and then declare, "This is from God," in order to sell it for a paltry price. Woe to them because of what their own hands have written, and woe to them for what they have earned.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then woe to those who write the Book with their own hands, and then say:"This is from Allah," to traffic with it for miserable price!- Woe to them for what their hands do write, and for the gain they make thereby.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fawaylun lillatheena yaktuboona alkitaba biaydeehim thumma yaqooloona hatha min AAindi Allahi liyashtaroo bihi thamanan qaleelan fawaylun lahum mimma katabat aydeehim wawaylun lahum mimma yaksiboona

80.arabic: وَقَالُوا۟ لَن تَمَسَّنَا ٱلنَّارُ إِلَّآ أَيَّامًا مَّعْدُودَةً ۚ قُلْ أَتَّخَذْتُمْ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ عَهْدًا فَلَن يُخْلِفَ ٱللَّهُ عَهْدَهُۥٓ ۖ أَمْ تَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet they say: "The Fire will not touch us for more than a few days." Say.. "Have you so received a promise from God?" "Then surely God will not withdraw His pledge. Or do you impute things to God of which you have no knowledge at all?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And they said, “The fire will not touch us except for a certain number of days”; say, “Have you taken a covenant from Allah – then Allah will certainly not break His covenant – or do you say something concerning Allah what you do not know?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And they say, 'The Fire shall not touch us save a number of days.' Say: 'Have you taken with God a covenant? God will not fail in His covenant; or say you things against God of which you know nothing?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they say: Fire shall touch us not save for a few numbered days. Say thou: have ye taken a covenant with Allah, so that Allah shall not fail His covenant? or fabricate ye against Allah that which ye know not

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And they (Jews) say, "The Fire (i.e. Hell-fire on the Day of Resurrection) shall not touch us but for a few numbered days." Say (O Muhammad Peace be upon him to them): "Have you taken a covenant from Allah, so that Allah will not break His Covenant? Or is it that you say of Allah what you know not?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they say, “The Fire will not touch us except for a number of days.” Say, “Have you received a promise from God—God never breaks His promise—or are you saying about God what you do not know?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They also say, "The fire of Hell is not going to touch us, and even if it does at all, it will be only for a few days". Say, "Have you obtained a promise from Allah which He would not break? Or, do you attribute to Allah things you do not know? Why will not the fire of Hell touch you?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And they (Jews) say, "The Fire shall not touch us but for a few numbered days." Say (O Muhammad to them): "Have you taken a covenant from Allah, so that Allah will not break His covenant Or is it that you say of Allah what you know not"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And they say: The Fire (of punishment) will not touch us save for a certain number of days. Say: Have ye received a covenant from Allah - truly Allah will not break His covenant - or tell ye concerning Allah that which ye know not?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And they say, ‘The Fire shall not touch us except for a number of days.’ Say, ‘Have you taken a promise from Allah? If so, Allah will never break His promise. Do you ascribe to Allah what you do not know?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They say: 'The Fire will never touch us except for a number of days' Say: 'Did Allah make you such a promise for Allah never breaks His promise or do you say about Allah what you do not know'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they say, "Never will the Fire touch us, except for a few days." Say, "Have you taken a covenant with Allah? For Allah will never break His covenant. Or do you say about Allah that which you do not know?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They have said, "Hell fire will never harm us except for just a few days." (Muhammad), ask them, "Have you made such agreements with God Who never breaks any of His agreements or you just ascribe to Him that which you do not know?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they say: Fire shall not touch us but for a few days. Say: Have you received a promise from Allah, then Allah will not fail to perform His promise, or do you speak against Allah what you do not know?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They say, "The Fire is not going to touch us, and [even if it does], it will only be for a few days!" Say [to them], "Have you received a promise from God, for God never breaks His promise, or do you attribute something to God which you do not know?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And they say: "The Fire shall not touch us but for a few numbered days:" Say: "Have ye taken a promise from Allah, for He never breaks His promise? or is it that ye say of Allah what ye do not know?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqaloo lan tamassana alnnaru illa ayyaman maAAdoodatan qul attakhathtum AAinda Allahi AAahdan falan yukhlifa Allahu AAahdahu am taqooloona AAala Allahi ma la taAAlamoona

81.arabic: بَلَىٰ مَن كَسَبَ سَيِّئَةً وَأَحَـٰطَتْ بِهِۦ خَطِيٓـَٔتُهُۥ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Why, they who have earned the wages of sin and are enclosed in error, are people of Hell, where they will abide for ever.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Yes, why not?* The one who earns evil and his sin surrounds him; he is from the people of fire (hell); they will remain in it forever. (You will remain in the fire forever).

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Not so; whoso earns evil, and is encompassed by his transgression -- those are the inhabitants of the Fire; there they shall dwell forever.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O Yea! whosoever earnoth vice and his sin hath encompassed him, those shall be the fellows of the Fire, as abiders therein.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Yes! Whosoever earns evil and his sin has surrounded him, they are dwellers of the Fire (i.e. Hell); they will dwell therein forever.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Indeed, whoever commits misdeeds, and becomes besieged by his iniquities—these are the inmates of the Fire, wherein they will dwell forever.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Whoever earns evil and becomes engrossed in sin shall be doomed to Hell and abide therein for ever.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Yes! Whosoever earns evil and his sin has surrounded him, they are dwellers of the Fire (i.e. Hell); they will dwell therein forever.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Nay, but whosoever hath done evil and his sin surroundeth him; such are rightful owners of the Fire; they will abide therein.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly whoever commits misdeeds and is besieged by his iniquity—such shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they will remain in it [forever].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Indeed, he who earns evil and becomes engrossed in his sin, they are the people of the Fire; in it they shall remain for ever.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Yes, whoever earns evil and his sin has encompassed him - those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: There is no doubt that evil doers who are engulfed in sins are the companions of hell fire wherein they will live forever.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Yea! whoever earns evil and his sins beset him on every side, these are the inmates of the fire; in it they shall abide.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Truly, those who do evil and are encompassed by their misdeeds, shall be the inmates of the Fire. Therein they shall abide forever,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nay, those who seek gain in evil, and are girt round by their sins,- they are companions of the Fire: Therein shall they abide (For ever).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Bala man kasaba sayyiatan waahatat bihi khateeatuhu faolaika ashabu alnnari hum feeha khalidoona

82.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But those who believe and do good deeds are people of Paradise, and shall live there forever.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And those who believe and do good deeds – they are the People of Paradise; they will abide in it forever.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And those that believe, and do deeds of righteousness -- those are the inhabitants of Paradise; there they shall dwell forever.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And those who believe and do righteous works, --those shall be the fellows of the Garden, as abiders therein.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah - Islamic Monotheism) and do righteous good deeds, they are dwellers of Paradise, they will dwell therein forever.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As for those who believe and do righteous deeds—these are the inhabitants of Paradise, wherein they will dwell forever.

translations.author: Maududi
