translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: As for those to whom We gave the Book, they recognise the place (which has now been made giblah), as clearly as they recognise their own children. But some of them are knowingly concealing the truth.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Those to whom We gave the Scripture (Jews and Christians) recognise him (Muhammad or the Ka`bah at Makkah) as they recognize their sons. But verily, a party of them conceal the truth while they know it ـ [i.e., the qualities of Muhammad which are written in the Tawrah and the Injil.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Those unto whom We gave the Scripture recognise (this revelation) as they recognise their sons. But lo! a party of them knowingly conceal the truth.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Those whom We have given the Book recognize him just as they recognize their sons, but a part of them indeed conceal the truth while they know.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those to whom We gave the Book know him (Prophet Muhammad) as they know their own sons. But a party of them conceal the truth while they know.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those to whom We gave the Scripture know him as they know their own sons. But indeed, a party of them conceal the truth while they know [it].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Book (Bible), know you (Muhammad) just as a well as they know their sons. It is certain that some of them deliberately hide the truth.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those whom We have given the Book recognize him as they recognize their sons, and a party of them most surely conceal the truth while they know (it).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those to whom We have given the Book recognize it just as they recognize their own sons. But, some of them knowingly conceal the truth.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The people of the Book know this as they know their own sons; but some of them conceal the truth which they themselves know.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena ataynahumu alkitaba yaAArifoonahu kama yaAArifoona abnaahum wainna fareeqan minhum layaktumoona alhaqqa wahum yaAAlamoona

147.arabic: ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۖ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُمْتَرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The truth is from your Lord, so be not among those who are sceptics.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is the Truth from your Lord, therefore (O those who listen) beware – do not be in doubt.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: The truth comes from thy Lord; then be not among the doubters.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The truth is from thy Lord; be then thou not of the doubters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (This is) the truth from your Lord. So be you not one of those who doubt.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The truth is from your Lord, so do not be a skeptic.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is in fact a Commandment from your Lord; so you should not have any doubt concerning this.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: This is) the truth from your Lord. So be you not one of those who doubt.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: It is the Truth from thy Lord (O Muhammad), so be not thou of those who waver.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: This is the truth from your Lord; so do not be among the skeptics.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The truth comes from your Lord so do not be among the doubters.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The truth is from your Lord, so never be among the doubters.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Never doubt that the essence of truth comes from your Lord

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The truth is from your Lord, therefore you should not be of the doubters.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Truth is what comes from your Lord; therefore, do not be of those who doubt.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The Truth is from thy Lord; so be not at all in doubt.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alhaqqu min rabbika fala takoonanna mina almumtareena

148.arabic: وَلِكُلٍّ وِجْهَةٌ هُوَ مُوَلِّيهَا ۖ فَٱسْتَبِقُوا۟ ٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ ۚ أَيْنَ مَا تَكُونُوا۟ يَأْتِ بِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Each has a goal to which he turns. So strive towards piety and excel the others: God will bring you all together wheresoever you be. God has power over everything.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And each one has a direction towards which he inclines, therefore strive to surpass others in good deeds; Allah will bring you all together, wherever you may be; indeed Allah may do as He wills.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Every man has his direction to which he turns; so be you forward in good works. Wherever you may be, God will bring you all together; surely God is powerful over everything.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: For every one is a direction witherward he turnoth; so strive to be foremost in virtues. Wheresoever ye may be, Allah will bring you together. Verily Allah is over everything Potent.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: For every nation there is a direction to which they face (in their prayers). So hasten towards all that is good. Wheresoever you may be, Allah will bring you together (on the Day of Resurrection). Truly, Allah is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: To every community is a direction towards which it turns. Therefore, race towards goodness. Wherever you may be, God will bring you all together. God is capable of everything.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Everyone has a direction towards which he turns in Prayer: so try to excel one another in good works. Allah will find you wheresoever you be, for nothing is out of Allah's reach.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: For every nation there is a direction to which they face (in their prayers). So hasten towards all that is good. Wheresoever you may be, Allah will bring you together (on the Day of Resurrection). Truly, Allah is able to do all things.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And each one hath a goal toward which he turneth; so vie with one another in good works. Wheresoever ye may be, Allah will bring you all together. Lo! Allah is Able to do all things.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Everyone has a cynosure to which he turns; so take the lead in all good works. Wherever you may be, Allah will bring you all together. Indeed Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And for everyone is a direction for which he turns. So race in goodness. And wherever you are, Allah will bring you all together. He has power over all things.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: For each [religious following] is a direction toward which it faces. So race to [all that is] good. Wherever you may be, Allah will bring you forth [for judgement] all together. Indeed, Allah is over all things competent.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Every one pursues his goal. Compete with each other in performing good deeds. Wherever you are, God will bring you all together. God has power over all things.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And every one has a direction to which he should turn, therefore hasten to (do) good works; wherever you are, Allah will bring you all together; surely Allah has power over all things.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Each community has its own direction in which it turns: vie, then, with one another in doing good works. Wherever you may be, God will bring you all before Him. God has power over all things.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: To each is a goal to which Allah turns him; then strive together (as in a race) Towards all that is good. Wheresoever ye are, Allah will bring you Together. For Allah Hath power over all things.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walikullin wijhatun huwa muwalleeha faistabiqoo alkhayrati ayna ma takoonoo yati bikumu Allahu jameeAAan inna Allaha AAala kulli shayin qadeerun

149.arabic: وَمِنْ حَيْثُ خَرَجْتَ فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ۖ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ ۗ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Wherever you come from turn towards the Holy Mosque: This in truth is from your Lord. God is not negligent of all you do.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And wherever you come from, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque; and indeed it is the truth from your Lord; and Allah is not unaware of your deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: From whatsoever place thou issuest, turn thy face towards the Holy Mosque; it is the truth from thy Lord. God is not heedless of the things you do.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And from whencesoever thou goest forth, turn thy face toward the Sacred Mosque, and verily it is the very truth from thy Lord; and Allah is not neglectful of that which ye work.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And from wheresoever you start forth (for prayers), turn your face in the direction of Al-Masjid-al-Haram (at Makkah), that is indeed the truth from your Lord. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And wherever you come from, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque. This is the truth from your Lord, and God is not heedless of what you do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: At whatever place you may be, turn your face towards Masjid Haram (at Prayer time) for this is, in fact, a Commandment of your Lord, and Allah is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And from wheresoever you start forth (for prayers), turn your face in the direction of Al-Masjid Al-Haram (at Makkah), that is indeed the truth from your Lord. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And whencesoever thou comest forth (for prayer, O Muhammad) turn thy face toward the Inviolable Place of Worship. Lo! it is the Truth from thy Lord. Allah is not unaware of what ye do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Whencesoever you may go out, turn your face towards the Holy Mosque. Indeed it is the truth from your Lord, and Allah is not oblivious of what you do.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: From wherever you emerge, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque. This is surely the truth from your Lord. Allah is never inattentive of what you do.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So from wherever you go out [for prayer, O Muhammad] turn your face toward al- Masjid al-Haram, and indeed, it is the truth from your Lord. And Allah is not unaware of what you do.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), wherever you go, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque (in Mecca). This is the truth from your Lord who is not unaware of what you do

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And from whatsoever place you come forth, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque; and surely it is the very truth from your Lord, and Allah is not at all heedless of what you do.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Wherever you set out from, turn your face in the direction of the Sacred Mosque -- this is the truth from your Lord: He is not unaware of what you do --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: From whencesoever Thou startest forth, turn Thy face in the direction of the sacred Mosque; that is indeed the truth from the Lord. And Allah is not unmindful of what ye do.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wamin haythu kharajta fawalli wajhaka shatra almasjidi alharami wainnahu lalhaqqu min rabbika wama Allahu bighafilin AAamma taAAmaloona

150.arabic: وَمِنْ حَيْثُ خَرَجْتَ فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ ۚ وَحَيْثُ مَا كُنتُمْ فَوَلُّوا۟ وُجُوهَكُمْ شَطْرَهُۥ لِئَلَّا يَكُونَ لِلنَّاسِ عَلَيْكُمْ حُجَّةٌ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ فَلَا تَخْشَوْهُمْ وَٱخْشَوْنِى وَلِأُتِمَّ نِعْمَتِى عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Whichever place you come from turn towards the Holy Mosque, and wherever you are, turn your faces towards it so that people may have no cause for argument against you, except such among them as are wicked. But do not fear them, fear Me that I may accomplish My favours on you, and you may find the right way perchance.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) wherever you come from, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque; and wherever you may be, O Muslims, turn your faces towards it only, so that people may not have an argument against you – except those among them who do injustice; therefore do not fear them, and fear Me; and this is in order that I complete My favour upon you and that you may attain guidance. –

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: From whatsoever place thou issuest, turn thy face towards the Holy Mosque; and wherever you may be, turn your faces towards it, that the people may not have any argument against you, excepting the evildoers of them; and fear you them not, but fear you Me; and that I may perfect My blessing upon you, and that haply so you may be guided;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And from whencesoever thou goest forth, turn thy face toward the Sacred Mosque, and wheresoever ye be, turn your face toward it, lest there should be with people an argument against you, except those of them who do wrong so fear them not, but fear Me; so that I may accomplish My favour upon you, and that ye may remain guided-

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And from wheresoever you start forth (for prayers), turn your face in the direction of Al-Masjid-al-Haram (at Makkah), and wheresoever you are, turn your faces towards, it (when you pray) so that men may have no argument against you except those of them that are wrong-doers, so fear them not, but fear Me! - And so that I may complete My Blessings on you and that you may be guided.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And wherever you come from, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque. And wherever you may be, turn your faces towards it. So that the people may not have any argument against you—except those who do wrong among them. So do not fear them, but fear Me, that I may complete My blessings upon you, and that you may be guided.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: At whatever place you may be, you must turn your face towards the Masjid Haram, and wherever you may be, you must turn your face towards the same at prayer so that people might not find an argument against you. As for the unjust people, they will never stop talking; so do not fear them but fear Me -- (Do this) so that I may complete My favour upon you and you may find the way to real success,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And from wheresoever you start forth (for prayers), turn your face in the direction of Al-Masjid Al-Haram (at Makkah), and wheresoever you are, turn your faces towards it (when you pray) so that men may have no argument against you except those of them that are wrongdoers, so fear them not, but fear Me! ـ And so that I may complete My blessings on you and that you may be guided.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Whencesoever thou comest forth turn thy face toward the Inviolable Place of Worship; and wheresoever ye may be (O Muslims) turn your faces toward it (when ye pray) so that men may have no argument against you, save such of them as do injustice - Fear them not, but fear Me! - and so that I may complete My grace upon you, and that ye may be guided.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And whencesoever you may go out, turn your face towards the Holy Mosque, and wherever you may be, turn your faces towards it, so that the people may have no allegation against you, neither those of them who are wrongdoers. So do not fear them, but fear Me, that I may complete My blessing on you and so that you may be guided.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: From wherever you emerge, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque, and wherever you are, face towards it, so that the people will have no argument against you, except the harmdoers among them. Do not fear them, fear Me, so that I will perfect My Favor to you and that you will be guided.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And from wherever you go out [for prayer], turn your face toward al-Masjid al-Haram. And wherever you [believers] may be, turn your faces toward it in order that the people will not have any argument against you, except for those of them who commit wrong; so fear them not but fear Me. And [it is] so I may complete My favor upon you and that you may be guided.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad) wherever you go, turn your face to the Sacred Mosque and Muslims, wherever you are, turn your faces in the same direction so that no group of people, except the unjust among them, would have any reason against you and so that I may establish My commandments for your people to have proper guidance. (The unjust may criticize you) but do not fear them, fear only Me.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And from whatsoever place you come forth, turn your face towards the Sacred Mosque; and wherever you are turn your faces towards it, so that people shall have no accusation against you, except such of them as are unjust; so do not fear them, and fear Me, that I may complete My favor on you and that you may walk on the right course.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: wherever you come from, turn your face to the Sacred Mosque; wherever you may be, turn your faces towards it, so that people will not have any argument against you except for the wrongdoers among them. Do not fear them; fear Me, so that I may perfect My favour to you and you may be rightly guided.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So from whencesoever Thou startest forth, turn Thy face in the direction of the sacred Mosque; and wheresoever ye are, Turn your face thither: that there be no ground of dispute against you among the people, except those of them that are bent on wickedness; so fear them not, but fear Me; and that I may complete My favours on you, and ye May (consent to) be guided;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wamin haythu kharajta fawalli wajhaka shatra almasjidi alharami wahaythu ma kuntum fawalloo wujoohakum shatrahu lialla yakoona lilnnasi AAalaykum hujjatun illa allatheena thalamoo minhum fala takhshawhum waikhshawnee waliotimma niAAmatee AAalaykum walaAAallakum tahtadoona

151.arabic: كَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا فِيكُمْ رَسُولًا مِّنكُمْ يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا وَيُزَكِّيكُمْ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَيُعَلِّمُكُم مَّا لَمْ تَكُونُوا۟ تَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Even as We sent a messenger from among you to convey Our messages to you and cleanse you, and teach you the Book and the wisdom, and what you did not know;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The way We have sent to you a Noble Messenger from among you, who recites to you Our verses and purifies you, and teaches you the Book and sound wisdom*, and teaches you what you did not know. (The traditions / sayings of the Holy Prophet – peace and blessings be upon him).

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: as also We have sent among you, of yourselves, a Messenger, to recite Our signs to you and to purify you, and to teach you the Book and the Wisdom, and to teach you that you knew not.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Even as We have sent amidst you an apostle from amongst you, who rehearseth unto you Our revelations and purifieth you, and teacheth you the Book and wisdom, and teacheth you that which ye were not wont to know.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Similarly (to complete My Blessings on you) We have sent among you a Messenger (Muhammad SAW) of your own, reciting to you Our Verses (the Quran) and sanctifying you, and teaching you the Book (the Quran) and the Hikmah (i.e. Sunnah, Islamic laws and Fiqh - jurisprudence), and teaching you that which you used not to know.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Just as We sent to you a messenger from among you, who recites Our revelations to you, and purifies you, and teaches you the Book and wisdom, and teaches you what you did not know.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: just as (you have found from this: that) We sent the Messenger to you from among you, who recites to you Our Revelations; who purifies your lives; who instructs you in the Book and in Wisdom and teaches you those things that you did not know.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Similarly (to complete My blessings on you), We have sent among you a Messenger (Muhammad) of your own, reciting to you Our verses (the Qur'an) and purifying you, and teaching you the Book (the Qur'an) and the Hikmah (i.e., Sunnah, Islamic laws and Fiqh ـ jurisprudence), and teaching you that which you did not know.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Even as We have sent unto you a messenger from among you, who reciteth unto you Our revelations and causeth you to grow, and teacheth you the Scripture and wisdom, and teacheth you that which ye knew not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Even as We sent to you an apostle from among yourselves, who recites to you Our signs and purifies you, and teaches you the Book and wisdom, and teaches you what you did not know.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: As We have sent among you a Messenger (Prophet Muhammad) from yourselves, to recite to you Our verses and to purify you, who will teach you the Book and Wisdom, and teach you that of which you have no knowledge.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Just as We have sent among you a messenger from yourselves reciting to you Our verses and purifying you and teaching you the Book and wisdom and teaching you that which you did not know.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: As We have sent a Messenger from your own people to show you evidence about Me, to purify you from sins, to teach you the Book, give you wisdom and instruct you in that which you did not know,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Even as We have sent among you an Apostle from among you who recites to you Our communications and purifies you and teaches you the Book and the wisdom and teaches you that which you did not know.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Thus We have sent among you a Messenger of your own to recite Our revelations to you, purify you and teach you the Book and wisdom, and to teach you what you did not know.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: A similar (favour have ye already received) in that We have sent among you a Messenger of your own, rehearsing to you Our Signs, and sanctifying you, and instructing you in Scripture and Wisdom, and in new knowledge.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kama arsalna feekum rasoolan minkum yatloo AAalaykum ayatina wayuzakkeekum wayuAAallimukumu alkitaba waalhikmata wayuAAallimukum ma lam takoonoo taAAlamoona

152.arabic: فَٱذْكُرُونِىٓ أَذْكُرْكُمْ وَٱشْكُرُوا۟ لِى وَلَا تَكْفُرُونِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So, therefore, remember Me, and I shall remember you; and give thanks and do not be ungrateful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore remember Me, I will cause you to be spoken of and acknowledge My rights, and do not be ungrateful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So remember Me, and I will remember you; and be thankful to Me; and be you not ungrateful towards Me.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Remember Me wherefore, and I shall remember you: and unto Me give thanks, and be notingrate unto Me.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Therefore remember Me (by praying, glorifying, etc.). I will remember you, and be grateful to Me (for My countless Favours on you) and never be ungrateful to Me.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So remember Me, and I will remember you. And thank Me, and do not be ungrateful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So remember Me and I will remember you, and give thanks to Me and be not ungrateful.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Therefore remember Me (by praying, glorifying). I will remember you, and be grateful to Me (for My countless favors on you) and never be ungrateful to Me.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Therefore remember Me, I will remember you. Give thanks to Me, and reject not Me.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Remember Me and I will remember you, and thank Me, and do not be ungrateful to Me.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: So remember Me, I will remember you. Give thanks to Me and do not be ungrateful towards Me.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So remember Me; I will remember you. And be grateful to Me and do not deny Me.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: therefore, remember Me and I shall remember you. Thank Me and do not hide the truth about Me.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore remember Me, I will remember you, and be thankful to Me, and do not be ungrateful to Me.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So remember Me; I will remember you. Be thankful to Me and do not be ungrateful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then do ye remember Me; I will remember you. Be grateful to Me, and reject not Faith.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faothkuroonee athkurkum waoshkuroo lee wala takfurooni

153.arabic: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱسْتَعِينُوا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ وَٱلصَّلَوٰةِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O you who believe, seek courage in fortitude and prayer, for God is with those who are patient and persevere.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: O People who Believe! Seek help from patience and prayer; indeed Allah is with those who patiently endure.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: O all you who believe, seek you help in patience and prayer; surely God is with the patient.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: O O Ye who believe! seek help in patience and prayer; verily Allah is with the patient.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: O you who believe! Seek help in patience and As-Salat (the prayer). Truly! Allah is with As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: O you who believe! Seek help through patience and prayers. God is with the steadfast.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: O Believers, seek help with fortitude and Salat, for Allah is with those who show fortitude.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: O you who believe! Seek help in patience and As-Salah (the prayer). Truly, Allah is with As-Sabirin (the patient).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: O ye who believe! Seek help in steadfastness and prayer. Lo! Allah is with the steadfast.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: O you who have faith! Take recourse in patience and prayer; indeed Allah is with the patient.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Believers, seek assistance in patience and prayer, Allah is with those who are patient.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: O you who have believed, seek help through patience and prayer. Indeed, Allah is with the patient.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Believers, help yourselves (in your affairs) through patience and prayer; God is with those who have patience.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O you who believe! seek assistance through patience and prayer; surely Allah is with the patient.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: You who believe, seek help through patience and prayer; surely, God is with the steadfast.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O ye who believe! seek help with patient perseverance and prayer; for Allah is with those who patiently persevere.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ya ayyuha allatheena amanoo istaAAeenoo bialssabri waalssalati inna Allaha maAAa alssabireena

154.arabic: وَلَا تَقُولُوا۟ لِمَن يُقْتَلُ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ أَمْوَٰتٌۢ ۚ بَلْ أَحْيَآءٌ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تَشْعُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do not say that those who are killed in the way of God, are dead, for indeed they are alive, even though you are not aware.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And do not utter regarding those who are slain in Allah's cause as “dead”; in fact they are alive, but it is you who are unaware.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And say not of those slain in God's way, 'They are dead'; rather they are living, but you are not aware.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And say not of those who are slain in the way of Allah: dead. Nay, they are living, but ye perceive not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And say not of those who are killed in the Way of Allah, "They are dead." Nay, they are living, but you perceive (it) not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And do not say of those who are killed in the cause of God, “Dead.” Rather, they are alive, but you do not perceive.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And do not say of those who are slain in the way of Allah, "they are dead." In fact, they are alive but you do not perceive that life.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And say not of those who are killed in the way of Allah, "They are dead." Nay, they are living, but you perceive (it) not.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And call not those who are slain in the way of Allah "dead." Nay, they are living, only ye perceive not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do not call those who were slain in Allah’s way ‘dead.’ No, they are living, but you are not aware.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do not say that those killed in the Way of Allah are dead, they are alive, although you are unaware.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And do not say about those who are killed in the way of Allah, "They are dead." Rather, they are alive, but you perceive [it] not.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do not consider those who are slain for the cause of God to be dead. They are alive but you are unaware of them.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And do not speak of those who are slain in Allah's way as dead; nay, (they are) alive, but you do not perceive.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do not say that those who are killed in God's cause are dead; they are alive, but you are not aware of it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And say not of those who are slain in the way of Allah: "They are dead." Nay, they are living, though ye perceive (it) not.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala taqooloo liman yuqtalu fee sabeeli Allahi amwatun bal ahyaon walakin la tashAAuroona

155.arabic: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُم بِشَىْءٍ مِّنَ ٱلْخَوْفِ وَٱلْجُوعِ وَنَقْصٍ مِّنَ ٱلْأَمْوَٰلِ وَٱلْأَنفُسِ وَٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Be sure We shall try you with something of fear and hunger and loss of wealth and life and the fruits (of your labour); but give tidings of happiness to those who have patience,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We will surely test you with some fear and hunger, and with paucity of wealth and lives and crops; and give glad tidings to those who patiently endure. –

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Surely We will try you with something of fear and hunger, and diminution of goods and lives and fruits; yet give thou good tidings unto the patient

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We will surely prove you with aught of fear and hunger and diminution in riches and lives and fruits; and bear thou the glad tidings unto the patient;

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And certainly, We shall test you with something of fear, hunger, loss of wealth, lives and fruits, but give glad tidings to As-Sabirin (the patient ones, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We will certainly test you with some fear and hunger, and some loss of possessions and lives and crops. But give good news to the steadfast.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We will surely put you to trial by involving you in fear and hunger and by causing loss of property, life and earnings. And give good tidings to those who remain steadfast in these trials:

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And certainly, We shall test you with something of fear, hunger, loss of wealth, lives and fruits, but give glad tidings to As-Sabirin (the patient).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And surely We shall try you with something of fear and hunger, and loss of wealth and lives and crops; but give glad tidings to the steadfast,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We will surely test you with a measure of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth, lives, and fruits; and give good news to the patient

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We shall test you with something of fear and hunger, and decrease of goods, life and fruits. Give glad tidings to the patient,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth and lives and fruits, but give good tidings to the patient,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We shall test you through fear, hunger, loss of life, property, and crops. (Muhammad), give glad news to the people who have patience

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We will most certainly try you with somewhat of fear and hunger and loss of property and lives and fruits; and give good news to the patient,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We shall certainly test you with fear and hunger, and loss of property, lives and crops. Give good news to those who endure with fortitude.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Be sure we shall test you with something of fear and hunger, some loss in goods or lives or the fruits (of your toil), but give glad tidings to those who patiently persevere,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walanabluwannakum bishayin mina alkhawfi waaljooAAi wanaqsin mina alamwali waalanfusi waalththamarati wabashshiri alssabireena

156.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَآ أَصَـٰبَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّآ إِلَيْهِ رَٰجِعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who say when assailed by adversity: "Surely we are for God, and to Him we shall return."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Those who say when calamity befalls them, “Indeed we belong to Allah and indeed it is to Him we are to return.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: who, when they are visited by an affliction, say, 'Surely we belong to God, and to Him we return';

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Who, when there afflicteth them an affliction, say: verily we are Allah's, and verily unto Him we are returners.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Who, when afflicted with calamity, say: "Truly! To Allah we belong and truly, to Him we shall return."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who, when a calamity afflicts them, say, “To God we belong, and to Him we will return.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: when a misfortune comes to them, they say, "We are Allah's and we shall certainly return to Him,''

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Who, when afflicted with calamity, say: "Truly, to Allah we belong and truly, to Him we shall return."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Who say, when a misfortune striketh them: Lo! we are Allah's and lo! unto Him we are returning.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —those who, when an affliction visits them, say, ‘Indeed we belong to Allah and to Him do we indeed return.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: who, in adversity say: 'We belong to Allah and to Him we shall return'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Who, when disaster strikes them, say, "Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and in difficulty say, "We are the servants of God and to Him we shall all return".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Who, when a misfortune befalls them, say: Surely we are Allah's and to Him we shall surely return.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who say, when afflicted with a calamity, "We belong to God and to Him we shall return,"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Who say, when afflicted with calamity: "To Allah We belong, and to Him is our return":-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena itha asabathum museebatun qaloo inna lillahi wainna ilayhi rajiAAoona

157.arabic: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَٰتٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُهْتَدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: On such men are the blessings of God and His mercy, for they are indeed on the right path.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: These are the people upon whom are the blessings from their Lord, and mercy; and it is they who are on guidance.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: upon those rest blessings and mercy from their Lord, and those -- they are the truly guided.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: These! on them shall be benedictions from their Lord and His mercy, and these! they are the rightly guided.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They are those on whom are the Salawat (i.e. blessings, etc.) (i.e. who are blessed and will be forgiven) from their Lord, and (they are those who) receive His Mercy, and it is they who are the guided-ones.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Upon these are blessings and mercy from their Lord. These are the guided ones.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Their Lord will bestow great blessings and mercy upon them; such are the people who are rightly guided.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They are those on whom are the Salawat (i.e., who are blessed and will be forgiven) from their Lord, and (they are those who) receive His mercy, and it is they who are the guided ones.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Such are they on whom are blessings from their Lord, and mercy. Such are the rightly guided.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: It is they who receive the blessings of their Lord and [His] mercy, and it is they who are the [rightly] guided.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: On those will be prayers and mercy from their Lord, those are guided.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those are the ones upon whom are blessings from their Lord and mercy. And it is those who are the [rightly] guided.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It is they who will receive blessings and mercy from God and who follow the right guidance.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those are they on whom are blessings and mercy from their Lord, and those are the followers of the right course.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: are the ones who will have blessings and mercy from their Lord: it is they who are on the right path!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They are those on whom (Descend) blessings from Allah, and Mercy, and they are the ones that receive guidance.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Olaika AAalayhim salawatun min rabbihim warahmatun waolaika humu almuhtadoona

158.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلصَّفَا وَٱلْمَرْوَةَ مِن شَعَآئِرِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَمَنْ حَجَّ ٱلْبَيْتَ أَوِ ٱعْتَمَرَ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِ أَن يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِمَا ۚ وَمَن تَطَوَّعَ خَيْرًا فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَاكِرٌ عَلِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Truly Safa and Marwa are the symbols of God. Whoever goes on pilgrimage to the House (of God), or on a holy visit, is not guilty of wrong if he walk around them; and he who does good of his own accord will find appreciation with God who knows everything.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Undoubtedly Safa and Marwah* are among the symbols of Allah; so there is no sin on him, for whoever performs the Hajj (pilgrimage) of this House (of Allah) or the Umrah (lesser pilgrimage), to go back and forth between them; and whoever does good of his own accord, then (know that) indeed Allah is Most Appreciative (rewards virtue), the All Knowing. (These are 2 hillocks near the Holy Ka’aba)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Safa and Marwa are among the waymarks of God; so whosoever makes the Pilgrimage to the House, or the Visitation, it is no fault in him to circumambulate them; and whoso volunteers good, God is All-grateful, All-knowing

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily Safa and Marwa are of the landmarks of Allah; so whosoever maketh a pilgrimage to the House, or performeth the Umra, in him there is no fault if he walketh in-between the twain. And whosoever voluntarily doth good, then verily Allah is Appreciative, Knowing.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! As-Safa and Al-Marwah (two mountains in Makkah) are of the Symbols of Allah. So it is not a sin on him who perform Hajj or 'Umrah (pilgrimage) of the House (the Ka'bah at Makkah) to perform the going (Tawaf) between them (As-Safa and Al-Marwah). And whoever does good voluntarily, then verily, Allah is All-Recogniser, All-Knower.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Safa and Marwa are among the rites of God. Whoever makes the Pilgrimage to the House, or performs the Umrah, commits no error by circulating between them. Whoever volunteers good—God is Appreciative and Cognizant.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Indeed Safa and Marwah are among the emblems of Allah: it is, therefore, no sin for him, who performs Hajj or `Umrah to the House of AIIah, to run between the two hills; and Allah knows and appreciates him who does any good with a willing heart.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, As-Safa and Al-Marwah are of the symbols of Allah. So it is not a sin on him who performs Hajj or `Umrah (pilgrimage) of the House to perform Tawaf between them. And whoever does good voluntarily, then verily, Allah is All-Recognizer, All-Knower.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! (the mountains) As-Safa and Al-Marwah are among the indications of Allah. It is therefore no sin for him who is on pilgrimage to the House (of Allah) or visiteth it, to go around them (as the pagan custom is). And he who doeth good of his own accord, (for him) lo! Allah is Responsive, Aware.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed Safa and Marwah are among Allah’s sacraments. So whoever makes hajj to the House, or performs the ‘umrah, there is no sin upon him to circuit between them. Should anyone do good of his own accord, then Allah is indeed appreciative, all-knowing.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Safah and Marwah are the waymarks of Allah. Whoever performs the pilgrimage to the House or the visit, there shall be no guilt on him to circumambulate between both of them. And whoever volunteers good, Allah is Thankful, the Knower.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, as-Safa and al-Marwah are among the symbols of Allah. So whoever makes Hajj to the House or performs 'umrah - there is no blame upon him for walking between them. And whoever volunteers good - then indeed, Allah is appreciative and Knowing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Safa and Marwah (names of two places in Mecca) are reminders of God. It is no sin for one who visits the Sacred House (in Mecca) to walk seven times between (Safa and Marwah.) Whoever willingly does a good deed in obedience to God, will find God All-knowing and Fully Appreciative.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely the Safa and the Marwa are among the signs appointed by Allah; so whoever makes a pilgrimage to the House or pays a visit (to it), there is no blame on him if he goes round them both; and whoever does good spontaneously, then surely Allah is Grateful, Knowing.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Safa and Marwah are among the symbols set up by God; there is nothing wrong if anyone goes on a pilgrimage to the House, or performs a minor pilgrimage, in walking to and fro between them. Of anyone who does good of his own accord, God is appreciative, and aware.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Behold! Safa and Marwa are among the Symbols of Allah. So if those who visit the House in the Season or at other times, should compass them round, it is no sin in them. And if any one obeyeth his own impulse to good,- be sure that Allah is He Who recogniseth and knoweth.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna alssafa waalmarwata min shaAAairi Allahi faman hajja albayta awi iAAtamara fala junaha AAalayhi an yattawwafa bihima waman tatawwaAAa khayran fainna Allaha shakirun AAaleemun

159.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ مَآ أَنزَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَٱلْهُدَىٰ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا بَيَّنَّـٰهُ لِلنَّاسِ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ۙ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَلْعَنُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَيَلْعَنُهُمُ ٱللَّـٰعِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They who conceal Our signs and the guidance We have sent them and have made clear in the Book, are condemned of God and are condemned by those who are worthy of condemning.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed those who hide the clear proofs and the guidance which We sent down, after We made it clear to mankind in the Book – upon them is the curse of Allah and the curse of those who curse.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those who conceal the clear signs and the guidance that We have sent down, after We have shown them clearly in the Book -- they shall be cursed by God and the cursers,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who hide that which We have sent down of evidence and the guidance, after We have expounded its Unto mankind in the Book, these! - curseth them Allah, and shall curse them the cursers

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who conceal the clear proofs, evidences and the guidance, which We have sent down, after We have made it clear for the people in the Book, they are the ones cursed by Allah and cursed by the cursers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who suppress the proofs and the guidance We have revealed, after We have clarified them to humanity in the Scripture—those—God curses them, and the cursers curse them.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Indeed Allah curses, and the cursers, too, curse those who conceal the clear teachings and guidance We have sent down, after We have made these plain in the Book for the guidance of all mankind.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who conceal the clear proofs, evidences and the guidance, which We have sent down, after We have made it clear for the People in the Book, they are the ones cursed by Allah and cursed by the cursers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! Those who hide the proofs and the guidance which We revealed, after We had made it clear to mankind in the Scripture: such are accursed of Allah and accursed of those who have the power to curse.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed those who conceal what We have sent down of manifest proofs and guidance, after We have clarified it in the Book for mankind—they shall be cursed by Allah and cursed by the cursers,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who hide the clear verses and the guidance We have sent down after We have clarified them in the Book for the people shall be cursed by Allah and cursed by the cursers,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who conceal what We sent down of clear proofs and guidance after We made it clear for the people in the Scripture - those are cursed by Allah and cursed by those who curse,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who hide the authoritative proofs and the guidance that We have revealed, after it has been made clear for the People of the Book, will be condemned by God and those who have the right to condemn.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely those who conceal the clear proofs and the guidance that We revealed after We made it clear in the Book for men, these it is whom Allah shall curse, and those who curse shall curse them (too).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who conceal the evidence of the truth and the guidance We have revealed, after We have made them clear to people in the Scripture, will be rejected by God and so do others.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who conceal the clear (Signs) We have sent down, and the Guidance, after We have made it clear for the people in the Book,-on them shall be Allah's curse, and the curse of those entitled to curse,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena yaktumoona ma anzalna mina albayyinati waalhuda min baAAdi ma bayyannahu lilnnasi fee alkitabi olaika yalAAanuhumu Allahu wayalAAanuhumu allaAAinoona

160.arabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ تَابُوا۟ وَأَصْلَحُوا۟ وَبَيَّنُوا۟ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَأَنَا ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But those who repent and reform and proclaim (the truth), are forgiven, for I am forgiving and merciful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Except those who repent and do reform and disclose (the truth) – so I will accept their repentance; and I only am the Most Acceptor of Repentance, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: save such as repent and make amends, and show clearly -- towards them I shall turn; I turn, All-compassionate.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Save those that repent and amend, and make manifest the truth I these it is toward whom I relent. And I am the Relenting, the Merciful!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Except those who repent and do righteous deeds, and openly declare (the truth which they concealed). These, I will accept their repentance. And I am the One Who accepts repentance, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Except those who repent, and reform, and proclaim. Those—I will accept their repentance. I am the Acceptor of Repentance, the Merciful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: I, however, will forgive those of them who repent of it, mend their ways and make own what they were concealing: for I am very generous in accepting repentance and showing mercy.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Except those who repent and do righteous deeds, and openly declare (the truth which they concealed). These, I will accept their repentance. And I am the One Who accepts repentance, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Except those who repent and amend and make manifest (the truth). These it is toward whom I relent. I am the Relenting, the Merciful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: except such as repent, make amends, and clarify—those I shall pardon, and I am the All-clement, the All-merciful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: except those who repent, and mend (their deeds) and clarify. Those I shall accept them. I am the Receiver of repentance, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Except for those who repent and correct themselves and make evident [what they concealed]. Those - I will accept their repentance, and I am the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: However, I shall accept the repentance of those of them who repent for their sins, reform their manners, and preach the truth; I am All-forgiving and All-merciful.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Except those who repent and amend and make manifest (the truth), these it is to whom I turn (mercifully); and I am the Oft-returning (to mercy), the Merciful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But from those who repent and mend their ways and make known the truth, I will certainly accept their repentance: I am the Ever Relenting, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Except those who repent and make amends and openly declare (the Truth): To them I turn; for I am Oft-returning, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Illa allatheena taboo waaslahoo wabayyanoo faolaika atoobu AAalayhim waana alttawwabu alrraheemu

161.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَمَاتُوا۟ وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَعْنَةُ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But those who deny, and die disbelieving, bear the condemnation of God and the angels and that of all men,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed upon those who disbelieved, and died as disbelievers, is the curse of Allah and of the angels and of men combined.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But those who disbelieve, and die disbelieving -- upon them shall rest the curse of God and the angels, and of men altogether,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who disbelieve, and die while they are infidels, these it is on whom shall be the curse of Allah and the angels and mankind all.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who disbelieve, and die while they are disbelievers, it is they on whom is the Curse of Allah and of the angels and of mankind, combined.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But as for those who reject faith, and die rejecting—those—upon them is the curse of God, and of the angels, and of all humanity.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Those who adopted the way of disbelief and died as disbelievers, are accursed of Allah and of angels and of all mankind: they shall remain accursed for ever.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who disbelieve, and die while they are disbelievers, it is they on whom is the curse of Allah and of the angels and of mankind, combined.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! Those who disbelieve, and die while they are disbelievers; on them is the curse of Allah and of angels and of men combined.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed those who turn faithless and die while they are faithless—it is they on whom shall be the curse of Allah, the angels and all mankind.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But those who disbelieve, and die disbelieving shall incur the curse of Allah, the angels, and all people.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers - upon them will be the curse of Allah and of the angels and the people, all together,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who deny My existence and die with such attitude will be subject to the condemnation of God, the angels, and all people.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers, these it is on whom is the curse of Allah and the angels and men all;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who deny the truth, and die as deniers, on them shall be the curse of God and of angels and of men altogether.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who reject Faith, and die rejecting,- on them is Allah's curse, and the curse of angels, and of all mankind;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena kafaroo wamatoo wahum kuffarun olaika AAalayhim laAAnatu Allahi waalmalaikati waalnnasi ajmaAAeena

162.arabic: خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ لَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ وَلَا هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Under which they will live, and their suffering will neither decrease nor be respite for them.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They will remain in it forever; neither will the punishment be lightened for them, nor will they be given respite.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: therein dwelling forever; the chastisement shall not be lightened for them; no respite shall be given them.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They shall be abiders therein, the torment shall not be lightened on them, nor shall they be respited.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They will abide therein (under the curse in Hell), their punishment will neither be lightened, nor will they be reprieved.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They will remain under it forever, and the torment will not be lightened for them, and they will not be reprieved.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Their punishment shall not be lightened, nor shall they be reprieved.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They will abide therein (under the curse in Hell), their punishment will neither be lightened nor will they be reprieved.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They ever dwell therein. The doom will not be lightened for them, neither will they be reprieved.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will remain in it [forever] and their punishment shall not be lightened, nor will they be granted any respite.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They are there (in the Fire) for eternity neither shall the punishment be lightened for them; nor shall they be given respite.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Abiding eternally therein. The punishment will not be lightened for them, nor will they be reprieved.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will live condemned forever, will have no relief from the torment, and no attention will be paid to them.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Abiding in it; their chastisement shall not be lightened nor shall they be given respite.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Under it they shall remain forever; their punishment shall not be lightened, nor shall they be granted respite.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will abide therein: Their penalty will not be lightened, nor will respite be their (lot).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Khalideena feeha la yukhaffafu AAanhumu alAAathabu wala hum yuntharoona

163.arabic: وَإِلَـٰهُكُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۖ لَّآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Your God is one God; there is no god other than He, the compassionate, ever-merciful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Your God is One God; there is no God except Him – the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Your God is One God; there is no god but He, the All-merciful, the All-compassionate.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And the God of you all is one God; there is no God but he, the Compassionate, the Merciful.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And your Ilah (God) is One Ilah (God - Allah), La ilaha illa Huwa (there is none who has the right to be worshipped but He), the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Your God is one God. There is no god but He, the Benevolent, the Compassionate.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Your Deity is Allah alone: there is no deity save the All-Beneficent and All-Merciful Allah.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And your Ilah (God) is One Ilah (God ـ Allah), La ilaha illa Huwa (there is none who has the right to be worshipped but He), the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Your Allah is One Allah; there is no Allah save Him, the Beneficent, the Merciful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Your god is the One God; there is no god except Him, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Your God is One God. There is no god except He. He is the Merciful, the Most Merciful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And your god is one God. There is no deity [worthy of worship] except Him, the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Our Lord is the only Lord. There is no God but He, the Beneficent and Merciful.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And your God is one God! there is no god but He; He is the Beneficent, the Merciful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Your God is one God. There is no deity save Him. He is the Compassionate, the Merciful.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And your Allah is One Allah: There is no god but He, Most Gracious, Most Merciful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wailahukum ilahun wahidun la ilaha illa huwa alrrahmanu alrraheemu

164.arabic: إِنَّ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَـٰفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ وَٱلْفُلْكِ ٱلَّتِى تَجْرِى فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ بِمَا يَنفَعُ ٱلنَّاسَ وَمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مِن مَّآءٍ فَأَحْيَا بِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا وَبَثَّ فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ دَآبَّةٍ وَتَصْرِيفِ ٱلرِّيَـٰحِ وَٱلسَّحَابِ ٱلْمُسَخَّرِ بَيْنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Creation of the heavens and the earth, alternation of night and day, and sailing of ships across the ocean with what is useful to man, and the rain that God sends from the sky enlivening the earth that was dead, and the scattering of beasts of all kinds upon it, and the changing of the winds, and the clouds which remain obedient between earth and sky, are surely signs for the wise.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and in the continuous alternation of night and day, and the ships which sail the seas carrying what is of use to men, and the water which Allah sends down from the sky thereby reviving the dead earth and dispersing all kinds of beasts in it, and the movement of the winds, and the obedient clouds between heaven and earth – certainly in all these are signs for the intelligent.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: .. Surely in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of night and day and the ship that runs in the sea with profit to men, and the water God sends down from heaven therewith reviving the earth after it is dead and His scattering abroad in it all manner of crawling thing, and the turning about of the winds and the clouds compelled between heaven and earth -- surely there are signs for a people having understanding.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the alternation of night and day, and the ships that course upon the sea laden with that which profiteth mankind, and that which Allah sendeth down of water from the heaven and quickenoth the earth thereby after the death thereof; and scattereth therein of all kinds of moving creatures, and in the veering of the winds and the cloud subjected for service betwixt heaven and earth. are signs unto a people who understand.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! In the creation of the heavens and the earth, and in the alternation of night and day, and the ships which sail through the sea with that which is of use to mankind, and the water (rain) which Allah sends down from the sky and makes the earth alive therewith after its death, and the moving (living) creatures of all kinds that He has scattered therein, and in the veering of winds and clouds which are held between the sky and the earth, are indeed Ayat (proofs, evidences, signs, etc.) for people of understanding.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: In the creation of the heavens and the earth; in the alternation of night and day; in the ships that sail the oceans for the benefit of mankind; in the water that God sends down from the sky, and revives the earth with it after it had died, and scatters in it all kinds of creatures; in the changing of the winds, and the clouds disposed between the sky and the earth; are signs for people who understand.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (If they want a sign for the perception of this Reality) surety there are countless signs for those who use their common sense; they can see alternation of the night and day, in the ships that sail the ocean laden with cargoes beneficial to mankind, and in the rain-water which Allah sends down from the sky and thereby gives life to the earth after its death and spreads over it all kinds of animate creatures, in the blowing of the winds and in the clouds which obediently wait for orders between the sky and the earth.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and in the alternation of night and day, and the ships which sail through the sea with that which is of use to mankind, and the water (rain) which Allah sends down from the sky and makes the earth alive therewith after its death, and the moving (living) creatures of all kinds that He has scattered therein, and in the veering of winds and clouds which are held between the sky and the earth, are indeed Ayat (proofs, evidences, signs, etc.) for people of understanding.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! In the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the difference of night and day, and the ships which run upon the sea with that which is of use to men, and the water which Allah sendeth down from the sky, thereby reviving the earth after its death, and dispersing all kinds of beasts therein, and (in) the ordinance of the winds, and the clouds obedient between heaven and earth: are signs (of Allah's Sovereignty) for people who have sense.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed in the creation of the heavens and the earth, and the alternation of night and day, and the ships that sail at sea with profit to men, and the water that Allah sends down from the sky—with which He revives the earth after its death, and scatters therein every kind of animal—and the changing of the winds, and the clouds disposed between the sky and the earth, there are surely signs for a people who exercise their reason.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: In the creation of the heavens and the earth; in the alternation of night and day; in the ships that sail upon the sea with what is beneficial to the people; in the water which Allah sends down from the sky and with which He revives the earth after its death, and He spread in it from each moving (creation); in the movement of the winds, and in the clouds that are compelled between heaven and earth surely, these are signs for people who understand.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and earth, and the alternation of the night and the day, and the [great] ships which sail through the sea with that which benefits people, and what Allah has sent down from the heavens of rain, giving life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness and dispersing therein every [kind of] moving creature, and [His] directing of the winds and the clouds controlled between the heaven and the earth are signs for a people who use reason.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The creation of the heavens and the earth, the alternation of nights and days, the ships that sail in the sea for the benefit of the people, the water that God sends from the sky to revive the dead earth where He has scattered all kinds of animals, the winds of all directions, and the clouds rendered for service between the sky and the earth are all evidence (of His existence) for those who use their reason.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Most surely in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of the night and the day, and the ships that run in the sea with that which profits men, and the water that Allah sends down from the cloud, then gives life with it to the earth after its death and spreads in it all (kinds of) animals, and the changing of the winds and the clouds made subservient between the heaven and the earth, there are signs for a people who understand.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: In the creation of the heavens and the earth; in the alternation of night and day; in the ships that sail the ocean bearing cargoes beneficial to man; in the water which God sends down from the sky and with which He revives the earth after its death, scattering over it all kinds of animals; in the courses of the winds, and in the clouds pressed into service between earth and sky, there are indeed signs for people who use their reason.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Behold! in the creation of the heavens and the earth; in the alternation of the night and the day; in the sailing of the ships through the ocean for the profit of mankind; in the rain which Allah Sends down from the skies, and the life which He gives therewith to an earth that is dead; in the beasts of all kinds that He scatters through the earth; in the change of the winds, and the clouds which they Trail like their slaves between the sky and the earth;- (Here) indeed are Signs for a people that are wise.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna fee khalqi alssamawati waalardi waikhtilafi allayli waalnnahari waalfulki allatee tajree fee albahri bima yanfaAAu alnnasa wama anzala Allahu mina alssamai min main faahya bihi alarda baAAda mawtiha wabaththa feeha min kulli dabbatin watasreefi alrriyahi waalssahabi almusakhkhari bayna alssamai waalardi laayatin liqawmin yaAAqiloona

165.arabic: وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَتَّخِذُ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَندَادًا يُحِبُّونَهُمْ كَحُبِّ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَشَدُّ حُبًّا لِّلَّهِ ۗ وَلَوْ يَرَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ إِذْ يَرَوْنَ ٱلْعَذَابَ أَنَّ ٱلْقُوَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعَذَابِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And yet there are men who take others as compeers of God, and bestow on them love due to God; but the love of the faithful for God is more intense. If only the wicked could see now the agony that they will behold (on the Day of Resurrection), they will know that to God belongs the power entirely'. And the punishment of God is severe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And some people create for themselves Gods (objects of worship) other than Allah, with devotion (love) equal to the devotion of Allah; and the believers do not love anybody with love equal to the love of Allah; and what will be their state, when the punishment will be before the eyes of the unjust (disbelievers)? For all power belongs wholly to Allah, and because Allah’s punishment is very severe.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Yet there be men who take to themselves compeers apart from God, loving them as God is loved; but those that believe love God more ardently. O if the evildoers might see, when they see the chastisement, that the power altogether belongs to God, and that God is terrible in chastisement,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And of mankind are some that set up compeers; unto Allah they love them as with the love due to Allah. And those who believe are strongest in love of Allah. Would that those who do wrong saw, when they saw the torment, that verily power belonged wholly unto Allah, and that Allah was severe in requital -

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And of mankind are some who take (for worship) others besides Allah as rivals (to Allah). They love them as they love Allah. But those who believe, love Allah more (than anything else). If only, those who do wrong could see, when they will see the torment, that all power belongs to Allah and that Allah is Severe in punishment.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Yet among the people are those who take other than God as equals to Him. They love them as the love of God. But those who believe have greater love for God. If only the wrongdoers would realize, when they see the torment; that all power is God’s, and that God is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (In spite of such clear signs of the Oneness of Allah), there are people who set up equals and rivals with Allah and adore them with the adoration due to Allah. whereas the Believers adore Allah most ardently. Would that these transgressors could realize now what they will realize, when they will see the chastisement before them that power and authority wholly belong to Allah and that Allah is severe in punishment!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And of mankind are some who take (for worship) others besides Allah as rivals (to Allah). They love them as they love Allah. But those who believe, love Allah more (than anything else). If only, those who do wrong could see, when they will see the torment, that all power belongs to Allah and that Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Yet of mankind are some who take unto themselves (objects of worship which they set as) rivals to Allah, loving them with a love like (that which is the due) of Allah (only) - those who believe are stauncher in their love for Allah - Oh, that those who do evil had but known, (on the day) when they behold the doom, that power belongeth wholly to Allah, and that Allah is severe in punishment!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Among the people are those who set up compeers besides Allah, loving them as if loving Allah—but the faithful have a more ardent love for Allah—though the wrongdoers will see, when they sight the punishment, that power, altogether, belongs to Allah, and that Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And among the people are those who take to themselves rivals, other than Allah, loving them as Allah is loved. But those who believe are more loving to Allah. When the harmdoers see when they see the punishment because the power altogether belongs to Allah, and that Allah is firm in punishment,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [yet], among the people are those who take other than Allah as equals [to Him]. They love them as they [should] love Allah. But those who believe are stronger in love for Allah. And if only they who have wronged would consider [that] when they see the punishment, [they will be certain] that all power belongs to Allah and that Allah is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Some people consider certain things equal to God and love them just as one should love God. However, the strongest of the believers' love is their love of God. Had the unjust been able to reflect about their condition, when facing the torment, they would have had no doubt that to God belongs All-power and that He is stern in His retribution.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And there are some among men who take for themselves objects of worship besides Allah, whom they love as they love Allah, and those who believe are stronger in love for Allah and O, that those who are unjust had seen, when they see the chastisement, that the power is wholly Allah's and that Allah is severe in requiting (evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Yet there are some who set up equals with God and adore them with the adoration due to God, but those who believe love God most. If only the wrongdoers could see, as they will see when they face the punishment, that all power belongs to God, and that God is severe in punishment.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Yet there are men who take (for worship) others besides Allah, as equal (with Allah): They love them as they should love Allah. But those of Faith are overflowing in their love for Allah. If only the unrighteous could see, behold, they would see the penalty: that to Allah belongs all power, and Allah will strongly enforce the penalty.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wamina alnnasi man yattakhithu min dooni Allahi andadan yuhibboonahum kahubbi Allahi waallatheena amanoo ashaddu hubban lillahi walaw yara allatheena thalamoo ith yarawna alAAathaba anna alquwwata lillahi jameeAAan waanna Allaha shadeedu alAAathabi

166.arabic: إِذْ تَبَرَّأَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتُّبِعُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوا۟ وَرَأَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ وَتَقَطَّعَتْ بِهِمُ ٱلْأَسْبَابُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When those who were followed will disclaim those who followed them, and see the torment all ties between them shall be severed,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
